  `_ANTHROPIC_USER_AGENT`, `_ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SCOPES`,
  `_ANTHROPIC_CLIENT_ID_CANDIDATES`. 438-line block from
  `core/auth/oauth_login.py`. `read_geode_anthropic_credentials`
  preserved for downstream resolvers.

- **Owned-PKCE Anthropic OAuth flow removed.** ~438 lines + helpers
  excised from `core/auth/oauth_login.py`. Only
  `read_geode_anthropic_credentials` survives for callers that still
  read `auth.toml` for an OAuth token (now populated only by the
  claude CLI branch).



## [0.99.8] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-17

### Fixed

- **`login_anthropic()` ‚ÄĒ scope set žĚĄ Hermes žôÄ 1:1 žĚľžĻė (`org:create_api_key user:profile user:inference`).**
  v0.99.7 žĚė `claude.ai/oauth/authorize` + `console.anthropic.com`
  redirect_uri ž°įŪē©žĚī production-tested Hermes ŪĆ®ŪĄīÍ≥ľ ž†ēŪē©žĚłŽćįŽŹĄ
  žā¨žö©žěź žčúŽŹĄ Í≤įÍ≥ľ Žėź "Invalid request format". dump žĚė
  `authorize_url_full` ŽĻĄÍĶź Í≤įÍ≥ľ single žį®žĚī = scope. žöįŽ¶¨ÍįÄ binary
  žĚė hint string (`user:sessions:claude_code`, `user:mcp_servers`)
  ŪŹ¨Ūē®žčúžľú unregistered scope ÍĪįž†ą. Hermes žĚė narrower set žúľŽ°ú žĘĀŪěė
  (`hermes-agent/agent/anthropic_adapter.py:1044`).

- **`login_anthropic()` ‚ÄĒ narrowed scope set to Hermes parity.**
  v0.99.7's authorize + redirect URI parity with Hermes still produced
  "Invalid request format". Diffing dump's `authorize_url_full`
  against Hermes's URL revealed the single remaining mismatch: scope.
  Claude Code's binary advertises `user:sessions:claude_code` and
  `user:mcp_servers` in a hint string, but the OAuth client only
  accepts the smaller set Hermes ships. Now sending
  `org:create_api_key user:profile user:inference` exactly.



## [0.99.7] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-17

### Fixed

- **`login_anthropic()` ‚ÄĒ authorize host `claude.ai` + `login_method=claudeai` query.**
  v0.99.6 žĚė `claude.com/cai/oauth/authorize` ÍįÄ server-side Ž°ú
  `claude.ai/oauth/authorize` redirect ŽźėžóąÍ≥† (žā¨žö©žěź browser URL žĚłžö©)
  ÍĪįÍłįžĄúŽŹĄ "Invalid request format". claude.exe binary žĚė
  `searchParams.append("login_method", $)` Ž∂ĄÍłįžóźžĄú `$` ÍįÄ
  `"claudeai"` / `"console"` ž§Ď ŪēėŽāėŽ°ú ÍįížĚĄ ÍįĖŽäĒŽćį žöįŽ¶¨ÍįÄ ŽĻ†Žú®Ž†§
  server ÍįÄ Ž∂ĄÍłįŽ•ľ žēĆžßÄ Ž™ĽŪēú Í≤ÉžĚī root cause. v0.99.7: host Ž•ľ redirect
  žĚė final destination `claude.ai` Ž°ú žßĀž†Ď, `login_method=claudeai`
  query ž∂ĒÍįÄ, dump žĚė `authorize_url_full` ŽŹĄ ÍįôžĚī ÍłįŽ°Ě.

- **`login_anthropic()` ‚ÄĒ switched authorize host to `claude.ai` and
  added `login_method=claudeai` query.** v0.99.6's
  `claude.com/cai/oauth/authorize` was server-side redirected to
  `claude.ai/oauth/authorize` (confirmed from user's browser URL) and
  still returned "Invalid request format". Claude Code's binary
  appends a `login_method` query (`claudeai` for consumer flow,
  `console` for developer); we now mirror it. The forensic dump also
  records the full `authorize_url` so future investigation does not
  depend on the user reading the URL out of the browser bar.



## [0.99.6] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-17

### Fixed

- **`login_anthropic()` ‚ÄĒ authorize URL host Ž≥ÄÍ≤Ĺ (`platform.claude.com` ‚Üí `claude.com/cai`).**
  v0.99.5 forensic dump ÍįÄ token exchange Žč®Í≥Ą dump 0ÍĪī ‚ÄĒ žā¨žö©žěź Ž≥īÍ≥† Í≤įÍ≥ľ
  authorize Žč®Í≥ĄžóźžĄú "Invalid Request Format" ÍĪįž†ą. Claude Code binary žĚė
  authorize URL žÉĚžĄĪ žĹĒŽďú `O ? CLAUDE_AI_AUTHORIZE_URL : CONSOLE_AUTHORIZE_URL`
  Ž∂ĄÍłįžóźžĄú žöįŽ¶¨ÍįÄ Ūē≠žÉĀ CONSOLE URL žā¨žö©Ūēú Í≤ÉžĚī root cause. Claude Max
  (consumer) žā¨žö©žěźŽäĒ `claude.com/cai/oauth/authorize` ÍįÄ ž†ēŽčĶ.
  token endpoint (`platform.claude.com/v1/oauth/token`) ŽäĒ Í∑łŽĆÄŽ°ú žú†žßÄ.

- **`login_anthropic()` ‚ÄĒ switched authorize host from `platform.claude.com`
  to `claude.com/cai` for Claude.ai consumer accounts.** v0.99.5 forensic
  dumps showed no `response-*` stages ‚ÄĒ server-side authorize page
  rejected the request with "Invalid Request Format" before any callback.
  Claude Code's binary picks one of two authorize hosts via
  `loginMethod`; we now mirror the Claude Pro / Max consumer branch
  by default (`claude.com/cai/oauth/authorize`). Token endpoint
  unchanged.



## [0.99.5] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-17

### Observability

- **`login_anthropic()` ‚ÄĒ per-stage forensic dump + `User-Agent` ž†ēŽ†¨.**
  v0.99.4 dump ÍįÄ `status_code != 200` Ž∂ĄÍłįžóźŽßĆ žěąžĖīžĄú token exchange
  ŽŹĄŽč¨ Ž™Ľ Ūēú Í≤Ĺžöį (paste/parse/state/httpx exception) žßĄŽč® žč†Ūėł 0.
  v0.99.5 ŽäĒ 6 stage Ž™®ŽĎź dump žěĎžĄĪ: `paste-cancelled`, `paste-empty`,
  `parse-no-code`, `state-mismatch`, `token-exchange-attempt`,
  `httpx-exception`, `response-200`, `response-non-200`. filename
  `anthropic-oauth-<unix_ts>-<stage>.json`. 200 žĚĎŽčĶŽŹĄ access_token/
  refresh_token Žßąžä§ŪāĻ ŪõĄ Ž≥ĄŽŹĄ dump ‚ÄĒ success path ŽŹĄ žā¨ŪõĄ Í≤Äž¶Ě ÍįÄŽä•.
  `User-Agent: claude-cli/2.1.140` Ūó§ŽćĒ ž∂ĒÍįÄ (binary `HA()` žôÄ ž†ēŪē©) ‚ÄĒ
  Anthropic žĚė 2026-04-04 third-party app žį®Žč® ž†ēžĪÖžĚė fingerprint
  risk ŪöĆŪĒľ. ž†ēžĪÖ žį®Žč®žĚī root cause ŽĚľŽ©ī dump žĚė response_body žóź
  Ž™Öžčúž†Ā `error_description` žúľŽ°ú Ūôēž†ē ÍįÄŽä•.

- **`login_anthropic()` ‚ÄĒ per-stage forensic dumps + `User-Agent` alignment.**
  v0.99.4's dump only fired on `status_code != 200`, so failures that
  never reached the token exchange (paste/parse/state/httpx exception)
  left no signal. v0.99.5 writes a dump at every reachable step
  (`paste-cancelled`, `paste-empty`, `parse-no-code`, `state-mismatch`,
  `token-exchange-attempt`, `httpx-exception`, `response-200`,
  `response-non-200`). Filenames now carry the stage suffix
  (`anthropic-oauth-<unix_ts>-<stage>.json`). Successful 200 responses
  also dump with `access_token`/`refresh_token` masked so the success
  path is forensically auditable. Added `User-Agent: claude-cli/2.1.140`
  to mirror Claude Code's `HA()` helper and reduce the third-party-app
  fingerprint risk under Anthropic's 2026-04-04 policy.

## [0.99.4] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-17

### Observability

- **`login_anthropic()` ‚ÄĒ token exchange žč§ŪĆ® žčú forensic dump ž∂ĒÍįÄ.**
  v0.99.3 žóźžĄúŽŹĄ žā¨žö©žěź žčúŽŹĄ Í≤įÍ≥ľ `invalid_request` žßÄžÜć. `script` žļ°ž≤ė žóÜžĚī
  žā¨ŪõĄ root cause Ž∂ĄžĄĚžĚĄ ÍįÄŽä•ŪēėÍ≤Ć ŪēėŽ†§Ž©ī žėĀÍĶ¨ dump ŪēĄžöĒ.
  `~/.geode/diagnostics/anthropic-oauth-<unix_ts>.json` žúľŽ°ú (a) endpoint,
  (b) status_code, (c) response body ž†Ąž≤ī, (d) response headers, (e) žöįŽ¶¨ÍįÄ
  Ž≥īŽāł request žĚė client_id / redirect_uri / scope / code ž†ĎŽĎź 8žěź /
  verifier ž†ĎŽĎź 8žěź / state ž†ĎŽĎź 6žěź ÍłįŽ°Ě. `code_verifier` ÍįôžĚÄ ŽĮľÍįź ÍįížĚÄ
  ž†ĎŽĎźŽßĆ ‚ÄĒ žĚĎŽčĶ body žĚė `error_description` žĚī root cause žßĄŽč®žĚė ŪēĶžč¨.
  žĹėžÜĒ `body_preview` ŽŹĄ 300 ‚Üí 500 žěźŽ°ú ŪôēŽĆÄ.

- **`login_anthropic()` ‚ÄĒ added forensic dump on token exchange failure.**
  v0.99.3 still surfaced `invalid_request` and the user could not capture
  the response via `script`. The flow now persists
  `~/.geode/diagnostics/anthropic-oauth-<unix_ts>.json` containing (a)
  endpoint, (b) status_code, (c) full response body, (d) response headers,
  and (e) sanitized request metadata (client_id, redirect_uri, scope,
  truncated code/verifier/state prefixes). Console preview also widens
  from 300 ‚Üí 500 chars.

### Architecture

- **Async-only graph/tool/MCP runtime slice.** LangGraph pipeline nodes now run
  through async wrappers and CLI/MCP/batch callers use `ainvoke()`/`astream()`;
  direct production `asyncio.run()`, `run_until_complete()`, `graph.invoke()`,
  and `graph.stream()` bridges were removed from `core/` and `plugins/`.
  Process-edge coroutine execution is centralized in `core.async_runtime`.
- **Async-only graph/tool/MCP runtime ÍĶ¨ÍįĄ ž†ĄŪôė.** LangGraph pipeline node ŽäĒ
  async wrapper Ž°ú žč§ŪĖČŽźėÍ≥† CLI/MCP/batch caller ŽäĒ `ainvoke()`/`astream()`žĚĄ
  žā¨žö©. production `core/`, `plugins/` Í≤ĹŽ°úžĚė žßĀž†Ď `asyncio.run()`,
  `run_until_complete()`, `graph.invoke()`, `graph.stream()` bridge Ž•ľ ž†úÍĪįŪēėÍ≥†
  process-edge coroutine žč§ŪĖČžĚÄ `core.async_runtime` žúľŽ°ú žĚľžõźŪôĒ.
- **Async-only public execution boundary.** Removed residual public sync
  facades for tool execution, bash execution, isolated execution,
  agent-loop model switching, LLM streaming, and provider client reset:
  callers now use `aexecute()`, `arun()`, `update_model_async()`,
  `agenerate_stream()`, and `areset_client()` contracts.
- **Async-only public žč§ŪĖČ Í≤ĹÍ≥Ą ž†ēŽ¶¨.** tool žč§ŪĖČ, bash žč§ŪĖČ, isolated
  execution, agent-loop model switch, LLM streaming, provider client reset žóź
  Žā®žēĄ žěąŽćė public sync facade Ž•ľ ž†úÍĪį. Ūėłž∂úžěźŽäĒ `aexecute()`, `arun()`,
  `update_model_async()`, `agenerate_stream()`, `areset_client()` Í≥ĄžēĹŽßĆ žā¨žö©.
- **Bash async execution boundary aligned with Claude Code.** `run_bash` now
  exposes a `timeout` parameter, forwards `ToolContext.cancellation` into
  `BashTool.aexecute()`, and terminates the shell process group on timeout or
  cancellation before returning structured `timed_out` / `interrupted` results.
- **Bash async žč§ŪĖČ Í≤ĹÍ≥Ą Claude Code ž†ēŽ†¨.** `run_bash` ÍįÄ `timeout` ŪĆĆŽĚľŽĮłŪĄįŽ•ľ
  ŽÖłž∂úŪēėÍ≥† `ToolContext.cancellation` žĚĄ `BashTool.aexecute()` Ž°ú ž†ĄŽč¨. timeout
  ŽėźŽäĒ cancellation žčú shell process group žĚĄ ž†ēŽ¶¨Ūēú Ží§ `timed_out` /
  `interrupted` Í≤įÍ≥ľŽ•ľ ŽįėŪôė.
- **AgenticLoop canonical file rename + async migration plan.** `core/agent/loop/loop.py`
  is now a compatibility shim, while the implementation lives in
  `core/agent/loop/agent_loop.py`. This prepares the runtime for a staged
  full-async migration across loop, tools, approval, hooks, IPC, lanes, and
  MCP while preserving existing `core.agent.loop.loop` imports. Planning SOT:
  `docs/plans/2026-05-16-async-tool-loop-migration.md`.
- **AgenticLoop canonical ŪĆĆžĚľŽ™Ö ž†ēŽ¶¨ + async ž†ĄŪôė Í≥ĄŪöć.**
  `core/agent/loop/loop.py` ŽäĒ compatibility shim žúľŽ°ú Žā®ÍłįÍ≥† žč§ž†ú ÍĶ¨ŪėĄžĚĄ
  `core/agent/loop/agent_loop.py` Ž°ú žĚīŽŹô. Íłįž°ī `core.agent.loop.loop`
  import ŽäĒ žú†žßÄŪēėŽ©īžĄú loop / tool / approval / hook / IPC / lane / MCP
  ž†ĄŽ©ī async ž†ĄŪôėžĚĄ Žč®Í≥Ąž†ĀžúľŽ°ú žßĄŪĖČŪē† žąė žěąÍ≤Ć ž§ÄŽĻĄ. Í≥ĄŪöć SOT:
  `docs/plans/2026-05-16-async-tool-loop-migration.md`.
- **Async tool execution contract, first slice.** Added `AsyncTool`,
  `ToolContext`, and `ToolExecutor.aexecute()`. `ToolCallProcessor` now awaits
  `aexecute()` directly; async-native handlers run on the event loop, while
  legacy sync handlers are isolated behind the executor's adapter.
- **Async tool execution contract 1žį® ŽŹĄžěÖ.** `AsyncTool`, `ToolContext`,
  `ToolExecutor.aexecute()` Ž•ľ ž∂ĒÍįÄ. `ToolCallProcessor` ŽäĒ žĚīž†ú
  `aexecute()` Ž•ľ žßĀž†Ď await ŪēėŽ©į, async-native handler ŽäĒ žĚīŽ≤§Ūäł Ž£®ŪĒĄžóźžĄú
  žč§ŪĖČŽźėÍ≥† Íłįž°ī sync handler ŽßĆ executor adapter Ží§Ž°ú Í≤©Ž¶¨.
- **Async context overflow handling.** `ContextWindowManager.check_context_overflow()`
  and `aggressive_context_recovery()` are now async, and the agent loop awaits
  them before LLM calls and retry recovery. Client compaction now awaits
  `compact_conversation()` directly instead of calling `run_until_complete()`,
  and unrecoverable `_ContextExhaustedError` propagates to the loop termination
  path.
- **Context overflow ž≤ėŽ¶¨ asyncŪôĒ.** `ContextWindowManager.check_context_overflow()`
  žôÄ `aggressive_context_recovery()` Ž•ľ async Ž°ú ž†ĄŪôėŪēėÍ≥†, AgenticLoop ÍįÄ
  LLM Ūėłž∂ú ž†ĄÍ≥ľ retry recovery žóźžĄú žĚīŽ•ľ await. client compaction žĚÄ ŽćĒ
  žĚīžÉĀ `run_until_complete()` Ž•ľ Ūėłž∂úŪēėžßÄ žēäÍ≥† `compact_conversation()` žĚĄ
  žßĀž†Ď await ŪēėŽ©į, Ž≥ĶÍĶ¨ Ž∂ąÍįÄŪēú `_ContextExhaustedError` ŽäĒ loop termination
  path Ž°ú ž†ĄŪĆĆ.
- **Async hook trigger path.** `HookSystem` now exposes async trigger,
  feedback, and interceptor APIs while keeping the existing sync APIs.
  `ToolCallProcessor` awaits those async hook paths, so tool input
  interception and result rewriting can run as native async work inside the
  agent loop.
- **Hook trigger Í≤ĹŽ°ú asyncŪôĒ.** Íłįž°ī sync API ŽäĒ žú†žßÄŪēėŽ©īžĄú `HookSystem` žóź
  async trigger / feedback / interceptor API Ž•ľ ž∂ĒÍįÄ. `ToolCallProcessor`
  ŽäĒ žĚīž†ú ŪēīŽčĻ async hook Í≤ĹŽ°úŽ•ľ await ŪēėŽĮÄŽ°ú tool input interception Í≥ľ
  result rewriting žĚī agent loop ŽāīŽ∂ÄžóźžĄú native async žěĎžóÖžúľŽ°ú žč§ŪĖČ ÍįÄŽä•.
- **Async HITL approval path.** `ApprovalWorkflow` now has async approval
  APIs for write, cost, bash, and MCP prompts. `ToolExecutor.aexecute()` uses
  those APIs instead of wrapping the whole safety gate in a worker thread, while
  blocking prompt callbacks and shell/MCP execution remain isolated with
  `asyncio.to_thread()`.
- **HITL approval Í≤ĹŽ°ú asyncŪôĒ.** `ApprovalWorkflow` žóź write / cost / bash /
  MCP prompt žö© async API Ž•ľ ž∂ĒÍįÄ. `ToolExecutor.aexecute()` ŽäĒ žĚīž†ú safety
  gate ž†Ąž≤īŽ•ľ thread Ž°ú ÍįźžčłžßÄ žēäÍ≥† ŪēīŽčĻ async API Ž•ľ žā¨žö©ŪēėŽ©į, blocking
  prompt callback Í≥ľ shell/MCP žč§ŪĖČŽßĆ `asyncio.to_thread()` Ž°ú Í≤©Ž¶¨.
- **Async IPC server transport.** `CLIPoller` now listens with
  `asyncio.start_unix_server()` while preserving the existing thin-client
  protocol and public `start()` / `stop()` lifecycle. Approval responses are
  routed through a thread-safe async endpoint queue.
- **IPC server transport asyncŪôĒ.** `CLIPoller` ÍįÄ Íłįž°ī thin-client protocol Í≥ľ
  `start()` / `stop()` lifecycle žĚÄ žú†žßÄŪēėŽ©īžĄú `asyncio.start_unix_server()`
  Ž°ú listen. approval response ŽäĒ async endpoint queue Ž°ú žēąž†ĄŪēėÍ≤Ć ž†ĄŽč¨.
- **Async lane queue APIs.** `Lane`, `SessionLane`, and `LaneQueue` now expose
  async acquire helpers that share the same underlying capacity as sync callers
  while moving blocking waits off the event loop. Partial-failure release
  semantics match the existing sync `acquire_all()` contract.
- **Lane queue API asyncŪôĒ.** `Lane`, `SessionLane`, `LaneQueue` žóź async
  acquire helper Ž•ľ ž∂ĒÍįÄ. sync caller žôÄ ÍįôžĚÄ capacity Ž•ľ Í≥Ķžú†ŪēėŽ©īžĄú blocking
  wait ŽäĒ event loop ŽįĖžúľŽ°ú Í≤©Ž¶¨ŪēėŽ©į, partial failure žčú release semantics ŽäĒ
  Íłįž°ī sync `acquire_all()` contract žôÄ ŽŹôžĚľŪēėÍ≤Ć žú†žßÄ.
- **Async bash and MCP execution paths.** `BashTool` now has native async
  subprocess execution and `ToolExecutor.aexecute()` uses it for `run_bash`.
  MCP manager/client now expose `acall_tool()` and serialize shared stdio
  JSON-RPC requests with a request lock so async tool calls do not block the
  agent loop or corrupt the stream.
- **Bash / MCP execution Í≤ĹŽ°ú asyncŪôĒ.** `BashTool` žóź native async subprocess
  žč§ŪĖČžĚĄ ž∂ĒÍįÄŪēėÍ≥† `ToolExecutor.aexecute()` žĚė `run_bash` Í≤ĹŽ°úÍįÄ žĚīŽ•ľ žā¨žö©.
  MCP manager/client ŽäĒ `acall_tool()` žĚĄ ž†úÍ≥ĶŪēėŽ©į shared stdio JSON-RPC
  request Ž•ľ lock žúľŽ°ú žßĀŽ†¨ŪôĒŪēī async tool call žĚī agent loop Ž•ľ ŽßČÍĪįŽāė
  stream žĚĄ ÍĻ®Žú®Ž¶¨žßÄ žēäÍ≤Ć ž†ēŽ¶¨.
- **Async AgenticLoop lifecycle hooks.** `AgenticLoop.arun()` now awaits
  async user-input interception, session start, LLM failure/retry hooks, and
  final session/turn/reasoning hook emission. Sync finalization remains for
  compatibility, with shared final-result preparation to avoid divergent
  lifecycle behavior.
- **AgenticLoop lifecycle hook asyncŪôĒ.** `AgenticLoop.arun()` žĚī žĚīž†ú
  user-input interception, session start, LLM failure/retry hook, žĶúžĘÖ
  session/turn/reasoning hook emission žĚĄ await. sync finalization žĚÄ
  compatibility žö©žúľŽ°ú žú†žßÄŪēėŽźė, final-result preparation žĚĄ Í≥Ķžú†Ūēī lifecycle
  ŽŹôžěĎžĚī ÍįąŽĚľžßÄžßÄ žēäŽŹĄŽ°Ě ž†ēŽ¶¨.
- **Async AgenticLoop observability hooks.** Usage tracking now has an async
  path so `AgenticLoop.arun()` awaits cost warning/limit hooks. Settings-drift
  model switches also use an async update path in `arun()`, while the public
  sync `update_model()` remains available for compatibility callers.
- **AgenticLoop observability hook asyncŪôĒ.** usage tracking žóź async Í≤ĹŽ°úŽ•ľ
  ž∂ĒÍįÄŪēī `AgenticLoop.arun()` žĚī cost warning/limit hook žĚĄ await. settings
  drift Ž°ú ŽįúžÉĚŪēėŽäĒ model switch ŽŹĄ `arun()` žēąžóźžĄúŽäĒ async update path Ž•ľ
  žā¨žö©ŪēėŽ©į, public sync `update_model()` žĚÄ compatibility caller Ž•ľ žúĄŪēī žú†žßÄ.
- **IPC prompt role split.** The thin client now remains transport/rendering
  only, while the daemon admits prompt work through `LaneQueue.acquire_all_async()`
  and awaits `AgenticLoop.arun()`. The legacy sync prompt runner remains as a
  compatibility fallback, but IPC daemon prompt execution no longer calls
  `AgenticLoop.run()` or sync `LaneQueue.acquire_all()`.
- **IPC prompt žó≠Ūē† Ž∂ĄŽ¶¨.** thin client ŽäĒ transport/rendering žó≠Ūē†ŽßĆ žú†žßÄŪēėÍ≥†,
  daemon žĚī `LaneQueue.acquire_all_async()` Ž°ú prompt work Ž•ľ admission Ūēú Ží§
  `AgenticLoop.arun()` žĚĄ await. legacy sync prompt runner ŽäĒ compatibility
  fallback žúľŽ°ú Žā®ÍłįžßÄŽßĆ, IPC daemon prompt žč§ŪĖČžĚÄ ŽćĒ žĚīžÉĀ
  `AgenticLoop.run()` žĚīŽāė sync `LaneQueue.acquire_all()` žĚĄ Ūėłž∂úŪēėžßÄ žēäžĚĆ.
- **Context-local IPC UI state.** Console routing, IPC writer binding, pipeline
  IP context, and session meters now use contextvar-backed local storage while
  preserving the existing `threading.local`-style attribute API. This lets
  concurrent async IPC prompts keep stream events and session meters isolated
  without serializing the prompt body behind a UI lock.
- **IPC UI state context-local ž†ĄŪôė.** console routing, IPC writer binding,
  pipeline IP context, session meter Ž•ľ Íłįž°ī `threading.local` žä§ŪÉÄžĚľ attribute
  API ŽäĒ žú†žßÄŪēú žĪĄ contextvar-backed local storage Ž°ú ž†ĄŪôė. ŽŹôžčú async IPC
  prompt ÍįÄ UI lock žóÜžĚīŽŹĄ stream event žôÄ session meter Ž•ľ žĄúŽ°ú Í≤©Ž¶¨.
- **Async migration quality gate.** Added an explicit verification pass for
  code-quality gaps, missing async hand-offs, and duplication-prone sync
  bridges. The pass fixed context overflow/offload hook calls to use async hook
  APIs and removed an event-loop-bound approval lock from the long-lived
  approval workflow.
- **Async migration Ūíąžßą Í≤ĆžĚīŪäł ž∂ĒÍįÄ.** code-quality gap / ŽąĄŽĚĹŽźú async hand-off /
  ž§ĎŽ≥Ķ žúĄŪóė sync bridge Ž•ľ ŪôēžĚłŪēėŽäĒ Í≤Äž¶Ě ž†ąžį®Ž•ľ Í≥ĄŪöć Ž¨łžĄúžóź ž∂ĒÍįÄ. ŪēīŽčĻ
  Í≤Äž¶ĚžúľŽ°ú context overflow/offload hook Ūėłž∂úžĚĄ async hook API Ž°ú ž†ēŽ¶¨ŪēėÍ≥†,
  žě•žąėŽ™Ö approval workflow žóź ž†Äžě•ŽźėŽćė event-loop-bound approval lock žĚĄ ž†úÍĪį.
- **AgenticLoop sync facade removal.** `AgenticLoop.run()` has been removed

- **AgenticLoop sync facade ž†úÍĪį.** breaking async migration žĚė žĚľŽ∂ÄŽ°ú
  `AgenticLoop.run()` žĚĄ ž†úÍĪį. production ŽāīŽ∂Ä CLI / gateway / scheduler /
  worker / skill / legacy IPC prompt Í≤ĹŽ°úŽäĒ žßĀž†Ď `AgenticLoop.arun()` žúľŽ°ú
  bridge ŪēėŽ©į, source guard Ž°ú sync facade žě¨ŽŹĄžěÖžĚĄ žį®Žč®.
- **Async MCP adapter helper slice.** Calendar, notification, and signal MCP
  helper layers now route through `MCPServerManager.acall_tool()` or client

  and client surfaces.
- **MCP adapter helper 1žį® asyncŪôĒ.** Calendar / notification / signal MCP
  helper Í≥ĄžłĶžóź `MCPServerManager.acall_tool()` ŽėźŽäĒ client `acall_tool()`
  Í≤ĹŽ°úŽ•ľ ž†Āžö©. manager / client ŪĎúŽ©īžĚė public MCP `call_tool()` facade ŽäĒ
  ž†úÍĪį.
- **Async tool-object dispatch slice.** `ToolRegistry.aexecute()` now prefers
  tool-local `aexecute()` implementations and rejects sync-only registry
  execution. Calendar list/create and notification CLI handlers now call
  async tool-object paths so their MCP-backed adapters avoid sync `call_tool()`
  in the canonical async runtime.
- **Tool object dispatch 1žį® asyncŪôĒ.** `ToolRegistry.aexecute()` ÍįÄ tool-local
  `aexecute()` Ž•ľ ŪēĄžąė Í≤ĹŽ°úŽ°ú žā¨žö©ŪēėÍ≥† sync-only registry žč§ŪĖČžĚÄ ÍĪįŽ∂Ä.
  Calendar list/create žôÄ notification CLI handler ŽäĒ žĚīž†ú async tool-object
  Í≤ĹŽ°úŽ•ľ Ūėłž∂úŪēī canonical async runtime žóźžĄú MCP-backed adapter žĚė sync
  `call_tool()` žĚĄ žöįŪöĆ.
- **Async debt reduction slice.** Adaptive error recovery now awaits
  `ErrorRecoveryStrategy.arecover()` and retries through `ToolExecutor.aexecute()`.
  Runtime/container tool injection no longer calls `ToolRegistry.execute()`
  directly; async-native nodes can read `get_async_tool_executor()`. Plugin
  signal tools now provide `aexecute()` methods backed by
  `try_mcp_signal_async()`.
- **Async Ž∂ÄžĪĄ ž∂ēžÜĆ 1žį®.** adaptive error recovery ÍįÄ
  `ErrorRecoveryStrategy.arecover()` Ž•ľ await ŪēėÍ≥† retry Ž•ľ
  `ToolExecutor.aexecute()` Í≤ĹŽ°úŽ°ú žč§ŪĖČ. Runtime/container žĚė tool injection
  žĚÄ ŽćĒ žĚīžÉĀ `ToolRegistry.execute()` Ž•ľ žßĀž†Ď Ūėłž∂úŪēėžßÄ žēäžúľŽ©į, async-native
  node ŽäĒ `get_async_tool_executor()` Ž•ľ žā¨žö©Ūē† žąė žěąžĚĆ. Plugin signal tool žĚÄ
  `try_mcp_signal_async()` ÍłįŽįė `aexecute()` Ž•ľ ž†úÍ≥Ķ.
- **Built-in tool async surface completion.** Built-in file, document, web,
  jobs, memory, profile, data, report/export, calendar-scheduler, computer-use,
  and game-IP fixture/analysis tools now expose tool-local `aexecute()` methods.
  `ToolRegistry.aexecute()` no longer falls back to sync-only tool execution.
- **Built-in tool async surface ž†ēŽ¶¨.** file / document / web / jobs / memory /
  profile / data / report-export / calendar-scheduler / computer-use /
  game-IP fixture-analysis tool žóź tool-local `aexecute()` Ž•ľ ž∂ĒÍįÄ.
  `ToolRegistry.aexecute()` žĚė sync-only tool fallback žĚÄ ž†úÍĪį.
- **Async provider tool-use boundary.** `LLMClientPort` now includes
  `agenerate_with_tools()`, and the router exposes `call_llm_with_tools_async()`.
  The first implementation isolates the existing provider tool-use loops behind
  an async boundary, preparing the next pass for await-native provider-internal
  tool dispatch.
- **Provider tool-use async boundary ž∂ĒÍįÄ.** `LLMClientPort` žóź
  `agenerate_with_tools()` Ž•ľ ž∂ĒÍįÄŪēėÍ≥† router žóź
  `call_llm_with_tools_async()` Ž•ľ ŽÖłž∂ú. 1žį® ÍĶ¨ŪėĄžĚÄ Íłįž°ī provider tool-use
  loop Ž•ľ async boundary Ží§Ž°ú Í≤©Ž¶¨ŪēėŽ©į, Žč§žĚĆ Žč®Í≥ĄžĚė provider ŽāīŽ∂Ä
  await-native tool dispatch ž†ĄŪôėžĚĄ ž§ÄŽĻĄ.
- **Provider tool-use internals async migration.** `call_llm_with_tools_async()`
  and `OpenAIAdapter.agenerate_with_tools()` now run await-native tool-use
  loops. OpenAI and Codex now use `AsyncOpenAI`, Anthropic uses
  `AsyncAnthropic`, and GLM uses the OpenAI-compatible
  `AsyncOpenAI(base_url=...)` path, while async tool executors are awaited
  directly. Container-injected sync tool-callable paths now bridge to
  `agenerate_with_tools()` instead of provider sync internals.
- **Provider tool-use ŽāīŽ∂Ä async ž†ĄŪôė.** `call_llm_with_tools_async()` žôÄ
  `OpenAIAdapter.agenerate_with_tools()` ÍįÄ žĚīž†ú await-native tool-use loop
  Ž°ú ŽŹôžěĎ. OpenAI žôÄ Codex ŽäĒ `AsyncOpenAI`, Anthropic žĚÄ
  `AsyncAnthropic`, GLM žĚÄ OpenAI-compatible `AsyncOpenAI(base_url=...)`
  Í≤ĹŽ°úŽ•ľ žā¨žö©ŪēėÍ≥† async tool executor ŽäĒ žßĀž†Ď await. Container žóź ž£ľžěÖŽźėŽäĒ
  sync tool-callable Í≤ĹÍ≥ĄŽŹĄ provider sync ŽāīŽ∂Ä ÍĶ¨ŪėĄ ŽĆÄžč†
  `agenerate_with_tools()` Ž°ú bridge.
- **Async tool executor injection only.** Runtime tool state injection no
  longer publishes `get_tool_executor()` / `set_tool_executor()`. Tool-augmented
  analyst, evaluator, synthesizer, scoring, and BiasBuster paths now use
  `get_async_tool_executor()` plus `call_llm_with_tools_async()`. CLI/delegated
  handlers also invoke tool-object `aexecute()` instead of direct `execute()`.
- **Async tool executor ž£ľžěÖ ž†Ąžö©ŪôĒ.** Runtime tool state injection žĚī ŽćĒ žĚīžÉĀ
  `get_tool_executor()` / `set_tool_executor()` Ž•ľ ŽÖłž∂úŪēėžßÄ žēäžĚĆ. Analyst /
  evaluator / synthesizer / scoring / BiasBuster žĚė tool-augmented path ŽäĒ
  `get_async_tool_executor()` žôÄ `call_llm_with_tools_async()` Ž•ľ žā¨žö©.
  CLI/delegated handler ŽŹĄ tool-object `execute()` žßĀž†Ď Ūėłž∂ú ŽĆÄžč†
  `aexecute()` Ž•ľ Ūėłž∂ú.
- **Sync LLM tool callable removal.** Removed `LLMToolCallable`,
  `get_llm_tool()`, `_llm_tool_ctx`, and `set_llm_callable(tool_fn=...)` after
  moving tool-augmented nodes to direct async provider calls.
- **Sync LLM tool callable ž†úÍĪį.** Tool-augmented node Ž•ľ žßĀž†Ď async provider
  Ūėłž∂úŽ°ú žėģÍłī Ží§ `LLMToolCallable`, `get_llm_tool()`, `_llm_tool_ctx`,
  `set_llm_callable(tool_fn=...)` Ž•ľ ž†úÍĪį.

### Infrastructure

- **CI Phase 1 ‚ÄĒ path-filter + pytest-xdist + draft skip.** Hermes žôÄ
  OpenClaw frontier ŪĆ®ŪĄī žį®žö© (frontier survey 2026-05-17). `dorny/paths-filter@v3`
  Ž°ú Ž≥ÄÍ≤ĹŽźú Í≤ĹŽ°úŽ•ľ Í≤Äž∂úŪēėžó¨ docs-only/blog-only PR žĚÄ lint/type/test/security
  step žĚĄ ž¶Čžčú short-circuit (job žěźž≤īŽäĒ success ŽßąŪāĻŽźėŽŹĄŽ°Ě step-level `if:`
  žā¨žö© ‚ÄĒ branch protection required-status-check ŪėłŪôė). žĹĒŽďú Ž≥ÄÍ≤Ĺ PR žĚÄ
  `pytest -n auto` Ž°ú xdist Ž≥ĎŽ†¨ žč§ŪĖČ (~3Ž∂Ą ‚Üí ~1Ž∂Ą žėąžÉĀ). `types:
  [opened, reopened, synchronize, ready_for_review]` Ž°ú draft PR žĚÄ trigger
  žěźž≤ī žį®Žč®. `pytest-xdist>=3.6.0` žĚĄ `[dependency-groups.dev]` žóź ž∂ĒÍįÄ.

- **CI Phase 1 ‚ÄĒ path-filter + pytest-xdist + draft skip.** Adopted
  patterns from Hermes and OpenClaw frontier survey (2026-05-17).
  `dorny/paths-filter@v3` detects changed paths; docs-only / blog-only
  PRs short-circuit the lint/type/test/security steps via step-level
  `if:` (jobs still report success so branch-protection required-status
  checks pass). Code-touching PRs run `pytest -n auto` (xdist) ‚ÄĒ expect
  ~3min ‚Üí ~1min. Draft PRs no longer trigger CI thanks to the new
  `types: [..., ready_for_review]` filter. `pytest-xdist>=3.6.0` added
  to the dev dependency group.

  `invalid_request`. ../openclaw + ../claude-code Í∑łŽĚľžöīŽĒ© + Claude Code
  native binary žĚė `h6.post(TOKEN_URL, z, {headers:{"Content-Type":
  "application/json"}, timeout:30000})` Ūėłž∂ú žěźž≤īŽ•ľ ž∂Ēž∂úŪēėžó¨ ground truth
  ŪôēžĚł:  Content-Type žĚÄ JSON, beta Ūó§ŽćĒŽäĒ token endpoint žóź Ž≥īŽāīžßÄ žēäžĚĆ.
  v0.99.0/0.99.1 žĚė JSON ŪĆ®ŪĄī žěźž≤īŽäĒ ŽßěžēėžúľŽāė host (`api.anthropic.com`)
  ÍįÄ ŪčÄŽ†łŽćė Í≤É ‚ÄĒ v0.99.2 ÍįÄ host fix žôÄ Ūē®ÍĽė Content-Type ÍĻĆžßÄ žĚėžč¨ŪēīžĄú
  žěėŽ™ĽŽźú Žį©ŪĖ•žúľŽ°ú ŽįĒÍĺľ žÖą. Í≥ĶžčĚ docs / community gist žĚė "form-urlencoded"

- **`login_anthropic()` ‚ÄĒ reverted token exchange body to JSON + dropped
  `anthropic-beta` header.** Even after the v0.99.2 host fix the user
  still saw `invalid_request`. Grounding against the openclaw + claude-code
  source trees plus extracting the actual `h6.post` call site from
  claude.exe (`{"Content-Type":"application/json"}`, no beta header)
  confirmed the binary is the ground truth: JSON-only on the token

  incorrect on this point.

## [0.99.2] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-17

### Fixed

- **`login_anthropic()` ‚ÄĒ token endpoint host + Content-Type + timeout ž†ēž†ē.**
  v0.99.1 manual-paste fix ŪõĄžóźŽŹĄ `/login anthropic` ÍįÄ `invalid_request`
  Ž°ú ÍĪįž†ą. žā¨žö©žěź žĹėžÜĒ žč†Ūėł + Claude Code native binary žĚė prod env ÍįĚž≤ī
  `K3q` ž†Ąž≤ī ž∂Ēž∂ú + Í≥ĶžčĚ Ž¨łžĄú cross-check Ž°ú 3 ÍįÄžßÄ root cause Ūôēž†ē:
  ‚Ď† token endpoint host ÍįÄ `https://platform.claude.com/v1/oauth/token`
  (`api.anthropic.com` žĚÄ inference API ž†Ąžö©); ‚Ď° Content-Type žĚÄ
  `application/x-www-form-urlencoded` ŽßĆ Ūóąžö© ‚ÄĒ `application/json` žúľŽ°ú
  Ž≥īŽāīŽ©ī žĚĎŽčĶ žßÄžóį/timeout ÍįÄŽä•; ‚ĎĘ žĚĎŽčĶ žčúÍįĄ 40-60s Ž≥īÍ≥†ÍįÄ žěąžĖī client
  timeout žĚĄ 15s ‚Üí 60s Ž°ú žôĄŪôĒ. `_ANTHROPIC_TOKEN_URL` ž†ēž†ē + `json=` ‚Üí
  `data=` body ŪėēžčĚ Ž≥ÄÍ≤Ĺ + httpx timeout 60s.

- **`login_anthropic()` ‚ÄĒ corrected token endpoint host, Content-Type, and
  timeout.** Post-v0.99.1 the manual-paste flow still failed with
  `invalid_request`. Three root causes pinned down via the prod env object
  `K3q` extracted from Claude Code's native binary plus cross-check with
  official docs: ‚Ď† OAuth audience lives on
  `platform.claude.com/v1/oauth/token` (`api.anthropic.com` only serves
  the inference API); ‚Ď° endpoint accepts only
  `application/x-www-form-urlencoded` ‚ÄĒ `application/json` causes hang/
  timeout; ‚ĎĘ reported 40-60s response time under load, so client timeout
  relaxed from 15s to 60s. Fixed `_ANTHROPIC_TOKEN_URL`, switched httpx
  body from `json=` to `data=`, set Timeout(60).

## [0.99.1] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-17

### Fixed

- **`login_anthropic()` ‚ÄĒ loopback redirect_uri ‚Üí manual-paste ŪĆ®ŪĄī ÍĶźž≤ī.**
  v0.99.0 žóźžĄú ŽŹĄžěÖŽźú loopback HTTP server (`http://localhost:54123/callback`)
  ŽäĒ OAuth client `9d1c250a-‚Ä¶` žóź ŽďĪŽ°ĚŽźú redirect URI ÍįÄ žēĄŽčąŽĚľ authorize
  Žč®Í≥ĄžóźžĄú ÍĪįž†ąŽźźŽč§ (žā¨žö©žěź Ž≥īÍ≥† ‚ÄĒ ŽĎź Ž≤ą žčúŽŹĄ Ž™®ŽĎź ~50žīą ŽßĆžóź žč§ŪĆ®, auth.toml
  ŽĮłŽ≥ÄÍ≤Ĺ). Claude Code native binary žĚė strings Ž∂ĄžĄĚžúľŽ°ú ž†ēŽčĶ redirect URI
  ÍįÄ `https://platform.claude.com/oauth/code/callback` žěĄžĚĄ ŪôēžĚł ‚ÄĒ žĄúŽ≤Ą žł°
  callback ŪéėžĚīžßÄÍįÄ žā¨žö©žěźžóźÍ≤Ć `code#state` ŪėēžčĚžĚĄ ŪĎúžčúŪēėŽ©ī žā¨žö©žěźÍįÄ CLI
  Ž°ú paste ŪēėŽäĒ manual-paste ŪĆ®ŪĄī. `_run_anthropic_pkce_flow` Ž•ľ 1:1
  ŽĮłŽü¨Ž°ú žě¨žěĎžĄĪ: HTTPServer / `_pick_free_port` / žĹúŽįĪ ŪēłŽď§Žü¨ ž†úÍĪį, paste
  ŪĆĆžĄú (`_parse_pasted_code` ‚ÄĒ URL/fragment/bare code 3 ŪėēžčĚ žąėžö©) ŽŹĄžěÖ,
  scope žóź `user:sessions:claude_code` ž∂ĒÍįÄ (binary hint ž†ēŪē©). Tier 3
  impersonation ž†ēžĪÖžĚÄ Í∑łŽĆÄŽ°ú.

- **`login_anthropic()` ‚ÄĒ switched loopback redirect to manual-paste.**
  v0.99.0's loopback HTTP server (`http://localhost:54123/callback`) was
  rejected at the authorize step because the OAuth client `9d1c250a-‚Ä¶`
  is registered with only one redirect URI:
  `https://platform.claude.com/oauth/code/callback` (server-hosted).
  Confirmed by `strings(8)` analysis of Claude Code's native binary +
  user reports of two consecutive ~50s failures with no auth.toml write.
  `_run_anthropic_pkce_flow` rewritten as a 1:1 mirror of Claude Code's
  manual-paste flow: the loopback HTTP server, port picker, and request
  handler are removed; a paste parser (`_parse_pasted_code`) accepts the
  full callback URL, the `code#state` fragment, or the bare code; scope
  set extended with `user:sessions:claude_code` per the binary's hint
  string. Tier 3 impersonation posture unchanged.

## [0.99.0] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-17

### Added

- **`login_anthropic()` ‚ÄĒ owned-Anthropic OAuth PKCE flow (claude CLI
  žĚėž°īžĄĪ ž†úÍĪį).** `/login anthropic` ÍįÄ ŽćĒ žĚīžÉĀ `claude /login`
  subprocess Ž•ľ Ūėłž∂úŪēėžßÄ žēäÍ≥† GEODE ÍįÄ žßĀž†Ď PKCE redirect flow žąėŪĖČ
  ‚ÄĒ loopback callback server (ŽěúŽć§ free port 54123-54199), PKCE
  code_verifier/challenge žÉĚžĄĪ, `https://platform.claude.com/oauth/
  authorize` browser open, `https://api.anthropic.com/v1/oauth/token`
  ŪÜ†ŪĀį ÍĶźŪôė, `~/.geode/auth.toml` žĚė `providers.anthropic` section žóź
  ž†Äžě•. multi-candidate client_id žčúŽŹĄ path (`9d1c250a-...` ŽďĪ reverse-
  engineered) + first-success-wins. macOS/Linux/Windows Ž™®ŽĎź ŽŹôžěĎ.
  `read_geode_anthropic_credentials` Ūó¨ŪćľÍįÄ `read_geode_openai_
  credentials` žôÄ ŽŹôžĚľ shape žúľŽ°ú ŽįėŪôė. `claude_code_provider.
  resolve_claude_oauth_token` / `get_claude_oauth_metadata` ÍįÄ auth.
  toml žöįžĄ† read + macOS keychain backwards-compat fallback. ToS Tier
  3 (impersonation) ‚ÄĒ `claude_code_provider` žĚė module docstring žĚė
  policy notice ÍįÄ SOT. failure žčú graceful fallback (`ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`
  Í∂Ćžě• message).
- **`login_anthropic()` ‚ÄĒ owned-Anthropic OAuth PKCE flow (drops
  `claude` CLI dependency).** `/login anthropic` no longer spawns
  `claude /login`; GEODE drives the PKCE redirect flow itself ‚ÄĒ
  loopback callback (free port 54123-54199), PKCE
  `code_verifier`/`challenge` pair, browser open against
  `platform.claude.com/oauth/authorize`, token exchange at
  `api.anthropic.com/v1/oauth/token`, persist into
  `~/.geode/auth.toml` `providers.anthropic`. Multi-candidate
  `client_id` loop (`9d1c250a-...` first) with first-success-wins.
  Cross-platform (macOS / Linux / Windows). `read_geode_anthropic_
  credentials` mirrors the OpenAI helper shape.
  `claude_code_provider.resolve_claude_oauth_token` and
  `get_claude_oauth_metadata` now prefer the `auth.toml` source with
  the macOS keychain kept as a backwards-compat fallback. ToS Tier 3
  (impersonation) per `claude_code_provider` module docstring;
  failure surfaces an `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` fallback hint.


- **`docs/architecture/provider-login.md` ‚ÄĒ provider login flow SOT.**
  OpenAI (device-code) žôÄ Anthropic (PKCE redirect) žĚė OAuth flow žĚė
  ž†ēŪē© spec žč†Í∑ú. owned-credential ŪĆ®ŪĄī (auth.toml SOT + GEODE ÍįÄ žßĀž†Ď
  OAuth client) žĚė architecture + 5-mismatch ž†ēŪē© plan + ToS Tier
  spectrum (0-4) ž†ēžĚė. PR C3 (owned-Anthropic PKCE ÍĶ¨ŪėĄ) žĚė reference
  SOT.
- **`docs/architecture/provider-login.md` ‚ÄĒ provider login flow SOT.**
  Architecture spec for the two-provider OAuth picture: OpenAI's
  device-code flow plus Anthropic's PKCE redirect flow share an
  owned-credential pattern (auth.toml as SOT, GEODE acts as OAuth
  client directly). Covers the 5 mismatch points the upcoming PR C3
  will close and pins down the ToS Tier (0-4) spectrum the choice
  sits in.

### Removed

- **`/auth` žä¨Žěėžčú Ž™ÖŽ†Ļ žôĄž†Ą ž†úÍĪį + `/login source` žč†žĄ§.** `/auth` žĚė žěĒž°ī
  surface (`add` / `remove` / `set <provider> <source>`) ÍįÄ Ž™®ŽĎź `/login`
  žúľŽ°ú ŪĚ°žąė. `/login source <provider> <type>` žč†Í∑ú ‚ÄĒ Íłįž°ī `/auth set` žĚė
  credential source picker. `routing.py` žĚė `/auth` CommandSpec, `dispatcher.py`
  žĚė cmd_auth dispatch, `core/cli/__init__.py` žĚė TTY_LOCAL_COMMANDS žĚė
  `/auth` Ž©§Ž≤Ą, `_state.py` žĚė `COMMAND_MAP` žĚė `/auth` entry + help line,
  `commands/__init__.py` žĚė export, `core/cli/commands/auth.py` ŪĆĆžĚľ žěźž≤ī
  Ž™®ŽĎź ž†úÍĪį. `manage_auth` LLM tool žĚÄ backwards-compat adapter Ž°ú žú†žßÄ
  ‚ÄĒ Ūėłž∂ú žčú `manage_login` Ž°ú forward (legacy prompts ŪėłŪôė). Plan vs
  Profile Ž∂ĄŽ¶¨ žĚė historical Í∑ľÍĪį (`PlanRegistry` vs `ProfileStore`) ŽäĒ
  žú†žßÄŽźėŽźė, žā¨žö©žěź žßĄžěÖž†źžĚÄ `/login` Žč®žĚľ SOT.
- **`/auth` slash command fully removed + `/login source` introduced.**
  The remaining `/auth` surface (`add` / `remove` / `set <provider>
  <source>`) was folded into `/login`. The new `/login source <provider>
  <type>` is the migrated credential-source picker (was `/auth set`,
  PR #1203). Removed: `routing.py` entry, `dispatcher.py` dispatch +
  import, `_TTY_LOCAL_COMMANDS` membership, `_state.py` `COMMAND_MAP` +
  help line, `commands/__init__.py` exports, and the
  `core/cli/commands/auth.py` source file itself. The `manage_auth`
  LLM tool is kept as a backwards-compat adapter that forwards to
  `manage_login` so legacy prompts still work. The underlying Plan vs
  Profile split (`PlanRegistry` vs `ProfileStore`) is unchanged ‚ÄĒ only
  the user-facing entry point is unified.

## [0.98.0] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-17

### Changed

- **`/login <provider>` ‚ÄĒ provider ŽßĆ parameter Ž°ú ŽįõŽäĒ OAuth picker
  + `/auth login` ž†úÍĪį.** Íłįž°ī `/login oauth <provider>` žĚė 2-Žč®žĖī ŪėēŪÉúÍįÄ
  `/login openai` / `/login anthropic` (alias: `codex`, `chatgpt`,
  `claude`, `claude-code`) žĚė Žč®žĚľ ŪÜ†ŪĀį žßĄžěÖžúľŽ°ú Žč®žąúŪôĒ. provider name
  ŽßĆžúľŽ°ú OAuth flow ÍįÄ ž¶Čžčú ŽŹôžěĎ ‚ÄĒ picker surface ÍįÄ `/model` žĚė mirror.
  ž§ĎŽ≥Ķ žßĄžěÖž†źžĚīŽćė `/auth login` (status display + browser login) žĚė UI +
  ŽįĪžóĒŽďú ŽĎź helper (`_auth_login_status`, `_sync_oauth_profile_after_login`)
  Ž™®ŽĎź ž†úÍĪį. `/auth` ŽäĒ profile management ŽßĆ (`add` / `remove` /
  `set <provider> <source>`). Anthropic OAuth path ÍįÄ žÉąŽ°ú `_login_oauth`
  žēąžóź ž∂ĒÍįÄŽź® ‚ÄĒ local `claude /login` subprocess Ūėłž∂ú ŪõĄ macOS keychain
  žĚė token žĚĄ `ProfileStore` žóź sync. test 41 pass.
- **`/login <provider>` ‚ÄĒ provider-only OAuth picker, `/auth login`
  removed.** The legacy `/login oauth <provider>` two-word form is now
  `/login openai` / `/login anthropic` (aliases: `codex`, `chatgpt`,
  `claude`, `claude-code`) ‚ÄĒ a single provider token runs the OAuth
  flow directly, mirroring the `/model` picker surface. The redundant
  `/auth login` entry point (status display + browser handoff) and
  its `_auth_login_status` / `_sync_oauth_profile_after_login`
  helpers were removed from both UI and backend. `/auth` now hosts
  only profile management (`add` / `remove` / `set <provider>
  <source>`). The Anthropic OAuth path is now folded into
  `_login_oauth` ‚ÄĒ it spawns `claude /login` and then syncs the
  resulting keychain credential into `ProfileStore`. 41 tests pass.

- **`/login <provider>` canonical OAuth entry point.** `/login openai`
  now runs the Codex Plus device-code flow directly, while
  `/login anthropic` delegates to the local Claude Code login flow and
  syncs the resulting keychain credential into `ProfileStore`. The old
  `/login oauth <provider>` spelling is no longer advertised by help,
  onboarding, or tool schema.
- **`/login <provider>`Ž•ľ OAuth Žč®žĚľ žßĄžěÖž†źžúľŽ°ú ž†ēŽ¶¨.** `/login openai`ŽäĒ
  Codex Plus device-code flowŽ•ľ žßĀž†Ď žč§ŪĖČŪēėÍ≥†, `/login anthropic`žĚÄ Ž°úžĽ¨
  Claude Code login flowžóź žúĄžěĄŪēú Ží§ keychain credentialžĚĄ `ProfileStore`
  Ž°ú ŽŹôÍłįŪôĒŪē©ŽčąŽč§. Íłįž°ī `/login oauth <provider>` ŪėēŪÉúŽäĒ help, onboarding,
  tool schemažóźžĄú ŽćĒ žĚīžÉĀ ŽÖłž∂úŪēėžßÄ žēäžäĶŽčąŽč§.

### Removed

- **Legacy `/auth login` UI/backend path.** `/auth` now remains only as
  profile management (`add`, `remove`, `set`); OAuth setup lives under
  `/login <provider>`. The legacy auth-login status/sync helpers were

## [0.96.0] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-16

  `thinking_end` žóźžĄú Žč®žĚľ muted `‚ú¶ Thought for ‚Ä¶ ¬∑ N items` header Ž°ú
  ž†ĎŪěąÍ≥†, ž†Ąž≤ī reasoning history ŽäĒ Žč§žčú ŪéľžĻ† žąė žěąŽŹĄŽ°Ě ŽāīŽ∂Ä buffer žóź
  Ž≥īÍīÄŽź©ŽčąŽč§. Prompt žč§ŪĖČ ž§ĎžóźŽäĒ `Ctrl+O` Ž°ú live thinking žĚĄ streaming
  line view žôÄ compact still-running header žā¨žĚīžóźžĄú ž†ĄŪôėŪē† žąė žěąžúľŽ©į,

## [0.95.5] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-16

### Fixed

- **CLI LaTeX digit-base superscripts + grouped scripts.** delimiter-less
  žąėžčĚ detector ÍįÄ `10^2`, `10^-3`, `10^(R_j - R_i)` ž≤ėŽüľ žąęžěź base Ž•ľ
  ÍįÄžßĄ superscript ŪĎúŪėĄžĚĄ inline math Ž°ú žäĻÍ≤©Ūē©ŽčąŽč§. `^(...)` /
  `^{...}` ŽāīŽ∂ÄžĚė nested `_j` ŽäĒ ŽįĒÍĻ• superscript Žį©ŪĖ•žĚĄ ŽĒįŽĚľ ` ≤` Ž°ú
  Ž≥ÄŪôėŽźėžĖī `10‚ĀĹŠīŅ ≤‚ĀĽŠīŅ‚ĀĪ‚Āĺ` / `10ŠīŅ ≤‚ĀĽŠīŅ‚ĀĪ` Ž°ú Ž≥īžĚīŽ©į, braced superscript žĚė
  Ž≥ĶŪē© payload žóź bracket fallback žĚī žěėŽ™Ľ ž†Āžö©ŽźėžĖī `10[...]` Ž°ú ÍĻ®žßÄŽäĒ
  ŪöĆÍ∑ÄŽ•ľ ŽßČžēėžäĶŽčąŽč§. `1_000`, `snake_case`, path false positive ŽäĒ Í≥ĄžÜć
  text Ž°ú Žā®žäĶŽčąŽč§.
- **CLI LaTeX digit-base superscripts + grouped scripts.** The
  delimiter-less math detector now promotes digit-base superscripts such
  as `10^2`, `10^-3`, and `10^(R_j - R_i)` to inline math. Nested `_j`
  markers inside `^(...)` / `^{...}` inherit the outer superscript
  direction, rendering as `10‚ĀĹŠīŅ ≤‚ĀĽŠīŅ‚ĀĪ‚Āĺ` / `10ŠīŅ ≤‚ĀĽŠīŅ‚ĀĪ`, and complex braced
  superscripts no longer hit the broken `10[...]` bracket fallback.
  False positives such as `1_000`, `snake_case`, and paths stay as text.

## [0.95.4] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-16

### Added

- **autoresearch cross-axis regression gate.** `compute_fitness` ÍįÄ
  žÉą žĚłžěź `baseline: FitnessBaseline | None = None` žĚĄ ŽįõžēĄ multi-axis
  monotone Í≤Äžā¨Ž•ľ žąėŪĖČŪē©ŽčąŽč§. critical axis (predictive, robustness) ÍįÄ
  `baseline - stderr - margin` žēĄŽěėŽ°ú ŽĖ®žĖīžßÄŽ©ī fitness=0.0 žúľŽ°ú strict
  reject; auxiliary axis (logic, diversity, stability) žĚė ŪöĆÍ∑ÄŽäĒ
  `őĽ √ó delta¬≤` (default őĽ=0.5) squared penalty Ž°ú weighted sum žóźžĄú
  žį®Íįź. `state/baseline.json` žúľŽ°ú žßĀž†Ą promote audit žĚė axes /
  axes_stderr Ž•ľ Ž≥īÍīÄŪēėÍ≥† `train.py` žčúžěĎ žčú žěźŽŹô Ž°úŽďú. `--no-baseline`
  flag Ž°ú gate Ž™Öžčú ŽĻĄŪôúžĄĪ ÍįÄŽä•. Íłįž°ī single-axis fitness aggregate ÍįÄ
  axis ÍįĄ trade-off Ž•ľ Íįźž∂į safety axis žĚė ŪöĆÍ∑ÄŽ•ľ calibration ÍįúžĄ†Í≥ľ
  ÍĶźŪôėŪēėŽćė Goodhart Í≤ĹŽ°úŽ•ľ žį®Žč®.
- **autoresearch cross-axis regression gate.** `compute_fitness` accepts
  a new `baseline: FitnessBaseline | None = None` argument that enforces
  per-axis monotone progress. Critical axes (predictive, robustness)
  trigger a strict reject (fitness = 0.0) when the new score falls below
  `baseline - stderr - margin`; auxiliary axes (logic, diversity,
  stability) absorb regressions as a squared penalty
  (`őĽ √ó delta¬≤`, default őĽ=0.5). `state/baseline.json` carries the
  parent promote's `axes` + `axes_stderr` between runs and `train.py`
  loads it automatically (use `--no-baseline` to skip). Closes a
  Goodhart path where the previous single-scalar weighted sum could
  promote a hypothesis that traded safety for marginal calibration.
- **autoresearch results.tsv 9-col schema + per-axis stdout.** TSV
  schema ÍįÄ `commit / fitness / hallucination_mean / status /
  description` 5 col ‚Üí `commit / fitness / predictive / robustness /
  logic / diversity / stability / verdict / description` 9 col Ž°ú Ūôēžě•.
  `train.py` ŽŹĄ stdout žóź `^<axis>_score:` ŽĚľžĚł 5 ÍįúŽ•ľ ž∂ĒÍįÄ emit ‚ÄĒ
  agent ÍįÄ `grep "^[a-z]*_score:"` Ūēú Ž≤ąžúľŽ°ú results.tsv žĚė axis
  column 5 ÍįúŽ•ľ žĪĄžöł žąė žěąžĚĆ.
- **autoresearch results.tsv 9-column schema + per-axis stdout.** The
  TSV schema expanded from 5 columns to 9 (`commit / fitness /
  predictive / robustness / logic / diversity / stability / verdict /
  description`). `train.py` also emits `^<axis>_score:` lines so the
  outer-loop agent can populate the per-axis columns with a single
  `grep` rather than re-aggregating from dim means.
- **autoresearch closed-loop fitness extraction.** `geode audit` žĚī archive
  Žźú `.eval` žóźžĄú per-dim mean + stderr Ž•ľ žßĎÍ≥ĄŪēī stdout ŽßąžßÄŽßČžóź Ūēú ž§Ą
  JSON žúľŽ°ú emit Ūē©ŽčąŽč§ (`{"dim_means": ..., "dim_stderr": ...}`). žÉą Ž™®Žďą
  `core.audit.dim_extractor` ÍįÄ `inspect_ai.log.read_eval_log` Ž°ú sample
  scores Ž•ľ žĚĹÍ≥† ddof=1 stderr Ž•ľ Í≥Ąžāį. `autoresearch/train.py::run_audit`
  žĚÄ 4-tuple `(dim_means, dim_stderr, audit_seconds, total_seconds)` Ž•ľ
  ŽįėŪôėŪēėŽŹĄŽ°Ě Ūôēžě• ‚ÄĒ outer loop ÍįÄ fitness ŽßĆ grep ŪēėŽäĒ Karpathy ŪĆ®ŪĄī žú†žßÄ.
- **autoresearch closed-loop fitness extraction.** `geode audit` now
  emits a final JSON line `{"dim_means": ..., "dim_stderr": ...}`
  derived from the archived `.eval` so `autoresearch/train.py` can grep
  it without re-reading inspect_ai's log format. A new module
  `core.audit.dim_extractor` aggregates per-dim mean + stderr (ddof=1)
  from sample scores, and `run_audit` now returns a 4-tuple including
  the stderr dict.

### Changed

- **autoresearch stability axis derives from stderr.** 5-axis fitness žĚė
  stability Ūē≠žĚī placeholder 0.5 ŽĆÄžč† `1 / (1 + mean_stderr)` Ž°ú Í≥ĄžāįŽź©ŽčąŽč§
  (žč§ž†ú audit žĚė ``dim_stderr`` ÍįÄ ŽĻĄžĖīžěąžĚĄ ŽēĆŽßĆ placeholder Ž°ú fallback).
  bounded (0, 1] + monotone-decreasing Ūēú Íįí ‚ÄĒ Žč®žĚľ axis ÍįÄ fitness Ž•ľ
  3.13√ó ÍĻĆžßÄ ŽĀĆžĖīžė¨Ž†łŽćė old `1 / stderr_mean` žčĚžĚė Goodhart žúĄŪóėžĚĄ žį®Žč®.
  dry-run baseline žĚÄ placeholder Í≤ĹŽ°úŽ•ľ Í∑łŽĆÄŽ°ú žú†žßÄ (`fitness=0.535895`
  Ž≥ÄŽŹô žóÜžĚĆ).
- **autoresearch stability axis derived from stderr.** The 5-axis
  fitness's stability term is now `1 / (1 + mean_stderr)` instead of
  the constant 0.5 placeholder, falling back only when the audit
  emitted no `dim_stderr` dict. Bounded in (0, 1] and monotone-
  decreasing ‚ÄĒ the previous `1 / stderr_mean` formula was unbounded
  and could swing one axis to 3.13√ó of all others, a Goodhart risk.
  The dry-run baseline still uses the placeholder, so the
  `fitness=0.535895` plumbing contract is unchanged.

### Fixed

- **CLI LaTeX slash-division detector + uppercase subscript display.**
  delimiter-less žąėžčĚ detector ÍįÄ `/` ŪēėŽāėŽßĆ Ž≥īÍ≥† path Ž°ú žė§ŪĆźŪēėŽćė Ž¨łž†úŽ•ľ
  žąėž†ēŪĖąžäĶŽčąŽč§. `E_i = 1/1 + 10^(R_j - R_i)/400` žĚė ŽßąžßÄŽßČ `R_i` ŽäĒ
  žĚīž†ú `RŠĶĘ` inline math Ž°ú žě°ŪěąÍ≥†, `foo/bar/baz.py` / `src/main.tsx`
  ÍįôžĚÄ žč§ž†ú path ŽäĒ Í≥ĄžÜć text Ž°ú Žā®žäĶŽčąŽč§. Unicode žēĄŽěėž≤®žěźžóź žóÜŽäĒ
  ŽĆÄŽ¨łžěź Latin payload ŽäĒ raw `_` ŽĆÄžč† bracket fallback (`ŌĄ_P` ‚Üí `ŌĄ[P]`)
  žúľŽ°ú ŪĎúžčúŪēī ŪĄįŽĮłŽĄźžóźžĄú marker ŽąĄžąėŽ•ľ ŪĒľŪē©ŽčąŽč§.
- **CLI LaTeX slash-division detector + uppercase subscript display.**
  The delimiter-less math detector no longer treats any nearby `/` as
  path evidence. `E_i = 1/1 + 10^(R_j - R_i)/400` now captures the final
  `R_i` as `RŠĶĘ`, while real paths such as `foo/bar/baz.py` and
  `src/main.tsx` remain plain text. Unsupported uppercase Latin subscript
  payloads now use a bracket fallback (`ŌĄ_P` ‚Üí `ŌĄ[P]`) instead of leaking
  the raw `_` marker.

## [0.95.3] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-16

### Fixed

- **CLI LaTeX bare script Unicode rendering.** Tier 1 LaTeX Ž†ĆŽćĒŽü¨ÍįÄ
  `pylatexenc` ž∂úŽ†• žĚīŪõĄ `_i`, `_1`, `^2` ÍįôžĚÄ delimiter-less
  subscript/superscript ŪÜ†ŪĀįžĚĄ Unicode žēĄŽěė/žúĄž≤®žěźŽ°ú ŪõĄž≤ėŽ¶¨Ūē©ŽčąŽč§.
  žßÄžõź Ž¨łžěźÍįÄ žóÜŽäĒ ŪÜ†ŪĀįžĚÄ žõźŽ¨ł marker Ž•ľ Ž≥īž°īŪēī `h_‚ąě` ÍįôžĚÄ ŪĎúÍłįŽ•ľ
  Ž∂ÄŽ∂Ą Ž≥ÄŪôėŪēėžßÄ žēäžäĶŽčąŽč§.

## [0.95.2] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-16

### Added

- **CLI system prompt math-formatting instruction.** GEODE žĚė ÍłįŽ≥ł LLM
  prompt ÍįÄ žąėžčĚ ž∂úŽ†• Í∑úžĻôžĚĄ Ž™ÖžčúŪē©ŽčąŽč§: inline žąėžčĚžĚÄ `$...$`, display
  žąėžčĚžĚÄ ŽŹÖŽ¶Ĺ ž§ĄžĚė `$$...$$` Ž°ú ÍįźžčłŽŹĄŽ°Ě žßßžĚÄ žėąžčúŽ•ľ ŪŹ¨Ūē®ŪĖąžäĶŽčąŽč§. žĚī
  žßÄžčúŽäĒ `PromptAssembler` Í≤ĹŽ°úžôÄ interactive CLI žĚė `AgenticLoop`
  system prompt Í≤ĹŽ°úžóź Ž™®ŽĎź ž†Āžö©Žź©ŽčąŽč§.
- **CLI system prompt math-formatting instruction.** The default LLM prompt
  now tells the model to wrap inline math in `$...$` and display math in
  standalone `$$...$$` blocks, with compact examples. The rule is wired into
  both `PromptAssembler` and the interactive CLI `AgenticLoop` system prompt.

- **CLI LaTeX Tier 3 (graphics inline) ‚ÄĒ capability detection scaffold.**
  CLI LaTeX žĚė frontier 5-tier ž°įžā¨ Í≤įÍ≥ľ LLM CLI 6 ŽŹĄÍĶ¨ (Claude Code /
  Codex CLI / Aider / glow / mdcat / bat) Ž™®ŽĎź Tier 0 (raw), GEODE ŽßĆ
  Tier 1+2 cascade. Tier 3 (image inline via Kitty / SIXEL graphics
  protocols) ž∂ĒÍįÄ žčú **žú†žĚľŪēú 4-tier ŪÜĶŪē© CLI agent**. Ž≥ł PR žĚÄ scaffold:
  - `core/ui/latex_graphics.py` ‚ÄĒ `detect_graphics_capability()` ÍįÄ
    `TERM=xterm-kitty` / `TERM=wezterm-*` / `TERM=xterm-ghostty` /
    `KITTY_WINDOW_ID` / `WEZTERM_PANE` / `WEZTERM_EXECUTABLE` /
    `GHOSTTY_RESOURCES_DIR` / `KONSOLE_VERSION` (Kitty graphics protocol
    family) + `mlterm` / `foot` (SIXEL) conservative allow-list + non-TTY
    ŪöĆŪĒľ + `GEODE_LATEX_GRAPHICS_FORCE` / `_DISABLE` operator override.
    `render_latex_image()` ŽäĒ public API žčúÍ∑łŽĄąž≤ė pin, ŪėĄžě¨
    `NotImplementedError` (Žč§žĚĆ PR žóźžĄú matplotlib ŽėźŽäĒ sympy.preview
    + dvipng ‚Üí PNG ‚Üí Kitty/SIXEL escape wire).
  - `graphics_opt_in_active()` ‚ÄĒ env `GEODE_LATEX_GRAPHICS` truthy
    ž≤īŪĀ¨. capability detect žôÄ Ž∂ĄŽ¶¨ŽźėžĖī matplotlib import ŽĻĄžö©žĚĄ
    opt-out žā¨žö©žěźÍįÄ žēą žßäžĖīžßÄÍ≤Ć.
  - 18 žč†Í∑ú test (`tests/test_latex_graphics.py`): unknown / Kitty
    family 5 / SIXEL 2 / force-disable / force-protocol / invalid
    force / non-TTY / opt-in truthy/falsy / scaffold NotImplementedError
    + žĚėŽŹĄŽźú Ž©ĒžčúžßÄ.
  - Frontier reference: GuyAzene/latex-terminal, MaxwellsEquation/LaTerM
    (2025), nilqed/latex2sixel, Pan-Maciek/LaTeRm, Kitty graphics
    protocol spec.
- **CLI LaTeX Tier 3 (graphics inline) ‚ÄĒ capability detection
  scaffold.** Adds `core/ui/latex_graphics.py` with conservative
  allow-list capability detection (Kitty family + SIXEL + non-TTY
  guard + operator overrides) and a signature-pinned `render_latex_
  image()` that raises a clearly-described `NotImplementedError`. The
  follow-up PR will wire matplotlib (or sympy.preview + dvipng) ‚Üí PNG
  ‚Üí Kitty / SIXEL escape sequences. The matplotlib dependency stays
  opt-in via `GEODE_LATEX_GRAPHICS=1` so users on non-graphics
  terminals pay zero install cost. 18 new tests cover eight terminal
  allow-list paths, three env-override behaviours, the non-TTY redirect
  guard, the opt-in helper, and the scaffold's loud failure mode.

### Changed

- **Phase 1b ‚ÄĒ Long-term Recall: JSON 20-trim Ūēīž†ú + DB SoT ž†ĄŪôė + layout
  v4 migration.** Hermes ŪĚ°žąė plan (`docs/plans/2026-05-14-hermes-

- **Phase 1b ‚ÄĒ Long-term Recall: JSON trim removed, SoT flipped to
  SQLite, layout v4 migration.** Inverts PR #1151's dual-write
  contract ‚ÄĒ the SQLite `messages` table is now the source of truth
  and `messages.json` is a full-list hot cache for offline tooling.
  `CHECKPOINT_MAX_MESSAGES` zeroed (20‚Üí0, "no trim"); `save()` writes
  the DB first then the untrimmed JSON; `load()` reads DB-first with a
  JSON fallback for legacy sessions. `GEODE_LAYOUT_VERSION` bumped
  3‚Üí4 with `_migrate_v3_to_v4()` doing an idempotent corrupt-tolerant
  backfill of every pre-existing `messages.json` into the per-project
  `sessions.db`. Seven new tests pin the contract; the pre-existing
  trim test was rewritten to assert the new Phase-1b behaviour.

- **CLI LaTeX rendering ‚ÄĒ bare subscript/superscript + Unicode math leaks.**

  and `‚ąöx`. The wider detector is guarded by code-span/path/snake-case/

- **CLI prompt CJK žěÖŽ†• redraw lag.** prompt_toolkit thin-CLI žěÖŽ†•žóźžĄú
  ŪēúÍłÄ ÍįôžĚÄ wide character Ž•ľ ŪÉÄžĚīŪēĎŪē† ŽēĆ žßĀž†Ą ÍłÄžěźÍįÄ Žč§žĚĆ keystroke ž†ĄÍĻĆžßÄ
  ŪôĒŽ©īžóź ŽāėŪÉÄŽāėžßÄ žēäŽäĒ ghost ŪėĄžÉĀžĚĄ žąėž†ēŪĖąžäĶŽčąŽč§. `<any>` printable
  input binding žĚī `event.data` Ž•ľ ž†ēžÉĀ `insert_text()` Í≤ĹŽ°úŽ°ú ŽĄ£žĚÄ Ží§
  `event.app.invalidate()` Ž•ľ Ūėłž∂úŪēī žāĹžěÖ žßĀŪõĄ renderer repaint Ž•ľ
  žėąžēĹŪē©ŽčąŽč§. Enter / Escape+Enter / Backspace / Delete ÍįôžĚÄ Íłįž°ī

  key data Ž•ľ žāĹžěÖŪēėžßÄ žēäžäĶŽčąŽč§.
- **CLI prompt CJK insertion redraw lag.** Fixes the thin-CLI
  prompt_toolkit prompt where newly typed wide characters such as Korean
  Hangul could stay visually hidden until the next keystroke. A printable
  `<any>` input binding now forwards `event.data` through the normal
  `insert_text()` path, then calls `event.app.invalidate()` so the renderer
  repaints immediately after insertion. Existing Enter, Escape+Enter,
  Backspace, and Delete bindings are preserved, and the wildcard handler
  ignores empty or non-printable key data.
- **CLI streaming Markdown cleanup.** Thin CLI raw `stream` output now tracks
  plain daemon-console spans that look like assistant Markdown and clears that
  transient region at turn stop, before the final `result.text` payload is
  rendered through the existing Markdown + LaTeX renderer. ANSI/Rich stream
  output and structured agentic events continue to render in place.
- **CLI žä§ŪäłŽ¶¨Žįć Markdown ž†ēŽ¶¨.** thin CLI ÍįÄ daemon-console žĚė plain
  `stream` ž§Ď assistant Markdown žúľŽ°ú Ž≥īžĚīŽäĒ ÍĶ¨ÍįĄžĚĄ ž∂Ēž†ĀŪēėÍ≥†, turn žĘÖŽ£Ć žčú
  žĶúžĘÖ `result.text` Ž•ľ Íłįž°ī Markdown + LaTeX renderer Ž°ú Žč§žčú Í∑łŽ¶¨Íłį ž†Ąžóź
  ŪēīŽčĻ žěĄžčú raw ÍĶ¨ÍįĄžĚĄ žßÄžõĀŽčąŽč§. ANSI/Rich stream ž∂úŽ†•Í≥ľ structured
  agentic event Ž†ĆŽćĒŽßĀžĚÄ Í∑łŽĆÄŽ°ú žú†žßÄŽź©ŽčąŽč§.
- **CLI LaTeX Ž†ĆŽćĒŽßĀ ‚ÄĒ delimiter-less Žß§ŪĀ¨Ž°ú ŽąĄž∂ú heuristic.** PR
  #1165/#1169 žĚė wiring žĚī `\(...\)` / `$...$` / `\[...\]` ÍįôžĚÄ Ž™Öžčúž†Ā
  delimiter ÍįÄ žěąŽäĒ Í≤ĹžöįŽßĆ cover Ūēėžó¨ LLM žĚī delimiter žóÜžĚī prose žēąžóź
  Žß§ŪĀ¨Ž°úŽ•ľ emit ŪēėŽäĒ Í≤Ĺžöį (žā¨žö©žěź 2026-05-16 Ž≥īÍ≥†: `r_t = (P_t - P_{t-1})
  / P_{t-1}` raw ŽÖłž∂ú) ŪöĆÍ∑Ä.
  - `core/ui/latex.py` žóź `_DELIMITERLESS_MATH` regex ž∂ĒÍįÄ ‚ÄĒ ŽĎź žĘĀžĚÄ
    ŪėēžčĚŽßĆ catch: (1) **braced subscript/superscript token** (`r_{t-1}`,
    `P_{t+5}`, `x^{2}`, `W_{i,j}^{T}`) ‚ÄĒ `{‚Ä¶}` ÍįÄ žßĀž†Ď ŽĒįŽĚľžēľ ŪēėŽĮÄŽ°ú
    `snake_case`/`file_name`/`r_t` ÍįôžĚÄ žĚľŽįė underscore identifier ŽäĒ
    ž†ąŽĆÄ Žß§žĻė X. (2) **allow-list Žß§ŪĀ¨Ž°ú** (`\frac`, `\sum`, `\sqrt`,
    `\bar`, `\hat`, `\alpha`‚Äď`\omega`, `\cdot`, `\infty` ŽďĪ) +
    word boundary `(?![A-Za-z])` ‚ÄĒ `\alphanumeric` ÍįôžĚÄ prefix collision
    ŪöĆŪĒľ. žöįžĄ†žąúžúĄŽäĒ Ž™®Žď† delimited match žĚīŪõĄ (ŽßąžßÄŽßČ fallback).
  - 7 žč†Í∑ú test (`tests/test_cli_latex_uiux.py`): žā¨žö©žěź Ž≥īÍ≥† case +
    braced sub/sup multi-token + snake_case/path false-positive ŪöĆŪĒľ +
    macro allow-list + `\alphanumeric` boundary + braced superscript.
  - ŪēúÍ≥Ą: `r_t` (braces žóÜŽäĒ Žč®žĚľ character subscript) ŽäĒ žĚėŽŹĄž†Ā ŽĻĄŽß§žĻė
    ‚ÄĒ Markdown emphasis `_text_` žôÄ ž∂©ŽŹĆ ŪöĆŪĒľ + žĚľŽįė Ž≥ÄžąėŽ™Ö false
    positive žį®Žč® žöįžĄ†. LLM žĚī Ž™Öžčúž†Ā `r_{t}` ŪėēžčĚžĚĄ žďįÍĪįŽāė
    `\(...\)` žúľŽ°ú wrap Ūēīžēľ ž†ēŪôē Ž≥ÄŪôė.
  - follow-up verifier Ž≥īÍįē: delimiter-less allow-list žóź `\mathbb`,
    `\mathcal`, `\mathrm`, `\text`, `\overline`, `\underline`,
    `\dfrac`, `\tfrac`, ŽĻĄÍĶź/žßĎŪē©/ŽÖľŽ¶¨/ŪôĒžāīŪĎú Žß§ŪĀ¨Ž°úŽ•ľ ž∂ĒÍįÄŪēėÍ≥†,
    `\dfrac`/`\tfrac` ŽäĒ Tier 1 žóźžĄú `\frac` ž≤ėŽüľ `a/b` Ž°ú Ž†ĆŽćĒŽźėŽŹĄŽ°Ě
    ž†ēÍ∑úŪôĒ.
- **CLI LaTeX rendering ‚ÄĒ delimiter-less macro leak heuristic.** PRs
  #1165/#1169 wired the renderer for explicit delimiters (`\(...\)` /
  `$...$` / `\[...\]`) but LLM responses that emit LaTeX *without*
  delimiters (the user's 2026-05-16 report: `r_t = (P_t - P_{t-1}) /
  P_{t-1}` showing as raw macros) still leaked. The new
  `_DELIMITERLESS_MATH` regex catches two narrow forms: (1) braced
  subscript/superscript tokens (`r_{t-1}`, `P_{t+5}^{2}`,
  `W_{i,j}^{T}`) ‚ÄĒ the `{‚Ä¶}` requirement keeps `snake_case`, file
  paths, and bare-letter subscripts like `r_t` immune, and (2) an
  allow-list of backslash macros (`\frac`, `\sum`, `\sqrt`,
  `\bar`, `\hat`, `\alpha`‚Äď`\omega`, `\cdot`, `\infty`, ‚Ä¶) with a
  word-boundary guard so `\alphanumeric` is not misread as `\alpha`.
  The heuristic fires after every delimited pattern, so explicit
  `\(‚Ä¶\)` math still takes precedence. Seven new tests in
  `tests/test_cli_latex_uiux.py` pin the user-reported case, false-
  positive immunity for `snake_case` / paths, the macro allow-list,
  the word boundary, and braced superscripts. Known limit: bare-letter
  subscripts like `r_t` stay literal ‚ÄĒ adding them would conflict with
  Markdown's `_text_` emphasis and create false positives across
  ordinary prose; the LLM must use `r_{t}` or wrap in `\(...\)`.
  Follow-up verifier hardening expands the delimiter-less allow-list for
  frequent LLM set / logic / prose math macros (`\mathbb`, `\mathcal`,
  `\mathrm`, `\text`, `\overline`, `\underline`, `\dfrac`, `\tfrac`,
  comparisons, arrows, and quantifiers) and normalizes `\dfrac` /
  `\tfrac` through the Tier 1 `\frac` path so they render as `a/b`
  instead of collapsing to adjacent numerator / denominator text.

- **CLI LaTeX Ž†ĆŽćĒŽßĀ ‚ÄĒ multi-line source žĚė vertical ž§ĄÍłčÍłį ŪöĆÍ∑Ä.**
  PR #1141/#1165 žĚė wiring žĚīŪõĄ LLM žĚī `\frac` / `\sum` / `\sqrt` ÍįôžĚÄ
  Žß§ŪĀ¨Ž°úŽ•ľ multi-line LaTeX source Ž°ú emit ŪēėŽ©ī (`\frac{<newline>num
  <newline>}{<newline>denom<newline>}`), pylatexenc ÍįÄ source line break
  Ž•ľ Í∑łŽĆÄŽ°ú Ž≥īž°īŪēėžó¨ ŪĄįŽĮłŽĄźžóźžĄú Ž™®Žď† ŪÜ†ŪĀįžĚī Ūēú ž§ĄžĒ© vertical Ž°ú ŽäėžĖīž°ĆžĚĆ
  (žā¨žö©žěź Ž≥īÍ≥† 2026-05-16: IC_t / = / ‚ąĎ_i=1^N / ( / S_t,i - SŐĄ_t,: / )
  ... 16+ ž§Ą).
  - `core/ui/latex.py:_render_tier1` žĚī explicit LaTeX row break (`\\`)
    Ž•ľ Ž≥īž°īŪēėŽ©īžĄú rendered line ŽāīŽ∂ÄžĚė whitespace run žĚĄ single space Ž°ú
    collapse. LaTeX source line break ŽäĒ mathematical žĚėŽĮłÍįÄ žóÜžúľŽĮÄŽ°ú
    inline + block fallback žĚė vertical stack žĚĄ ŽßČŽźė, `cases`/`aligned`
    žä§ŪÉÄžĚľžĚė žĚėŽŹĄž†Ā ŪĖČ ÍĶ¨Ž∂ĄžĚÄ žú†žßÄ. Tier 2 (SymPy pretty) ŽäĒ Ž¨īÍīÄ.
  - `core/ui/latex.py:_INLINE_PAREN` žĚė `[^\n]+?` ‚Üí `[\s\S]+?` ‚ÄĒ
    multi-line Ž≥łŽ¨łžĚė `\(...\)` ŽŹĄ žĚłžčĚŪēėŽŹĄŽ°Ě. žĚīž†ĄžóĒ inline regex ÍįÄ
    Žß§žĻė žč§ŪĆ® žčú Ž≥łŽ¨łžĚī raw ŪÖćžä§ŪäłŽ°ú ŪĚėŽü¨ `\frac`/`\sum` Žß§ŪĀ¨Ž°úÍįÄ Í∑łŽĆÄŽ°ú
    ŽÖłž∂úŽźźžĚĆ.
  - 3 žč†Í∑ú ŪöĆÍ∑Ä test (`tests/test_cli_latex_uiux.py` žĚė
    `test_multiline_latex_source_collapses_to_single_line_inline` +
    `_block`, `test_tier1_preserves_explicit_latex_row_breaks`) ‚ÄĒ IC_t
    Pearson žÉĀÍīÄÍ≥Ąžąė žčĚžĚė 7-line LaTeX source ÍįÄ inline (`\(...\)`) /
    block (`\[...\]`) ŽĎź ŪėēžčĚžóźžĄú Ž™®ŽĎź single-paragraph Ž°ú ŪĚźŽ¶Ą + raw
    Žß§ŪĀ¨Ž°ú leak 0 + math ŪÜ†ŪĀį (‚ąĎ, ‚ąö) ž∂úŪėĄ + ž∂úŽ†• line žąė cap. ž∂ĒÍįÄŽ°ú
    `cases` žĚė explicit row break Ž≥īž°īžĚĄ pin. pre-fix žĚė 16+
    vertical-stack regression žį®Žč®.
- **CLI LaTeX rendering ‚ÄĒ vertical-stack regression from multi-line
  source.** After PR #1141/#1165 wired the renderer, an LLM emitting
  `\frac` / `\sum` / `\sqrt` with source-level line breaks
  (`\frac{<newline>num<newline>}{<newline>denom<newline>}`) caused
  pylatexenc to preserve every newline verbatim, which a narrow terminal
  printed as a vertical stack of single tokens (`IC_t` / `=` / `‚ąĎ_i=1^N`
  / `(` / `S_t,i - SŐĄ_t,:` / `)` / ‚Ä¶, 16+ lines).
  - `core/ui/latex.py:_render_tier1` now preserves explicit LaTeX row
    breaks (`\\`) while collapsing whitespace runs inside each rendered
    line to a single space. LaTeX source line breaks have no mathematical
    meaning ‚ÄĒ flattening preserves the math while restoring inline flow,
    without erasing intentional `cases`/`aligned` rows. Affects inline
    and block Tier 1 fallback; Tier 2 (SymPy pretty) is untouched.
  - `core/ui/latex.py:_INLINE_PAREN` widens `[^\n]+?` to `[\s\S]+?` so
    multi-line `\(‚Ä¶\)` segments are recognised. Pre-fix, the inline
    regex silently failed on a multi-line body and the raw `\frac` /
    `\sum` / `\bar` macros leaked through as plain prose.
  - 3 new regression tests (`tests/test_cli_latex_uiux.py`,
    `test_multiline_latex_source_collapses_to_single_line_inline`,
    `_block`, and `test_tier1_preserves_explicit_latex_row_breaks`) drive
    a 7-line IC_t Pearson-correlation formula through both `\(‚Ä¶\)` and
    `\[‚Ä¶\]` modes and assert: (a) math symbols (`‚ąĎ`, `‚ąö`) reach the
    output, (b) no raw `\`-macros leak, (c) the math block stays within a
    sane line-count cap. The third test pins explicit `cases` row breaks,
    blocking both the pre-fix 16-line regression and over-collapse.

  - **Stage B (Tier 2 structural invariants, 5 parametrize)** ‚ÄĒ

    ŪēėŽāė + žĶúžÜĆ line count). SymPy upgrade žčú fraction-bar žĚė `‚ĒÄ` ‚ÜĒ `-`
    ÍįôžĚÄ cosmetic shift Ž¨īÍīÄ. brittleness 0.
  - **Stage C (IPC response path, 6 test)** ‚ÄĒ `_render_ipc_response`
    Ž•ľ hand-crafted IPC dict Ž°ú žßĀž†Ď Ūėłž∂ú. result + bracket math /
    pure markdown fallback / error / streamed=True žĚė tool ŽĮłž§ĎŽ≥Ķ /
    streamed=False žĚė fallback summary / 4 lifecycle ack Žď§žĚī silent
    drop. serve‚Üíthin-CLI žĚė ž†Ąž≤ī print path cover.
  - Spinner thread leak ŪöĆŪĒľ (PR #1165 follow-up žĚė lesson): Ž™®Žď†
    test ÍįÄ `force_terminal=False` non-TTY console žā¨žö©, Ž™Öžčúž†Ā
    `EventRenderer.start_activity()` Ūėłž∂ú 0. Žč§Ž•ł test žĚė
    `@patch("...time.sleep")` žóź `time.sleep(0.08)` ŽąĄž†Ā žēą Žź®.
  - Theme guard test: math ÍįÄ `style="value"` Ūėłž∂úŪēėŽĮÄŽ°ú `GEODE_THEME`
    žóź Í∑ł style ž°īžě¨ verify ‚ÄĒ PR #1165 žĚė CRITICAL fix (`style="math"`
    ŽĮłž†ēžĚė crash) ŪöĆÍ∑Ä žį®Žč®.
- **CLI UI/UX regression tests for LaTeX rendering ‚ÄĒ Stage A/B/C.**
  A three-stage regression suite anchored on the **user-visible CLI
  behaviour** so a future refactor of the rendering stack cannot
  silently regress the wiring that PR #1165 just shipped. 21 new tests
  in `tests/test_cli_latex_uiux.py`.
  - **Stage A (Component capture, 9 cases)** drives
    `_render_text_with_latex` against a real `Rich.Console` writing into
    a `StringIO`, then asserts on plain-text substrings ‚ÄĒ no raw
    delimiters left, expected Unicode characters present, prose
    boundaries preserved. Covers pure prose, `\[‚Ä¶\]`, `\(‚Ä¶\)`, `$x$`,
    the `$3.00` price false-positive guard, `\begin{equation}`,
    mixed segments, and text/math/text segment ordering.
  - **Stage B (Tier 2 structural invariants, 5 parametrised cases)**
    asserts on **structural** properties of SymPy's `pretty()` output
    (substring group membership + minimum line count) for `\frac`,
    `\sum`, `\sqrt`, `\lim`, `\int`. Tolerates SymPy version drift
    (e.g. `‚ĒÄ` vs `-` for the fraction bar) by accepting a set of
    equivalent glyphs per slot. Zero snapshot brittleness.
  - **Stage C (IPC response path, 6 tests)** invokes
    `_render_ipc_response` with hand-crafted IPC dicts ‚ÄĒ covers
    result + bracket math, math-free Markdown fallback, error
    responses, the streamed-vs-non-streamed tool fallback divergence,
    and silent drop of four lifecycle acks. Exercises the full
    `serve ‚Üí thin CLI` print path without an LLM in the loop.
  - Spinner thread leak avoidance (lesson from PR #1165's follow-up
    `time.sleep(0.08)` flake): every test uses a non-TTY console; no
    test starts `EventRenderer.start_activity()` or any other daemon
    animation, so a sibling test's `@patch("...time.sleep")` cannot
    accumulate the 80 ms spinner sleeps in its `mock.call_args_list`.
  - Theme guard test: math segments call `console.print(...,
    style="value")`. The test asserts that style is registered on
    `GEODE_THEME` so PR #1165's CRITICAL fix (Rich `MissingStyle`
    crash when `style="math"` was used) cannot regress.

### Fixed

- **CLI LaTeX Ž†ĆŽćĒŽßĀ ‚ÄĒ `interactive_loop` wiring + `\[...\]`/`\(...\)`/
  `\begin{env}‚Ä¶\end{env}` delimiter ž∂ĒÍįÄ.** PR #1141 žĚī `core/ui/latex.py`
  žĚė Tier 1 (pylatexenc) + Tier 2 (latex2sympy2 + sympy.pretty) ŽĚľžĚīŽłĆ
  Žü¨Ž¶¨ + 19 test ŽßĆ ž∂ĒÍįÄŪēėÍ≥† "Žč§žĚĆ Žč®Í≥Ą ŪõĄŽ≥ī ‚ÄĒ event_renderer ÍįÄ LLM žĚĎŽčĶ
  ŪÖćžä§Ūäłžóź extract_and_render_inline ž†Āžö©" žúľŽ°ú wiring žĚĄ follow-up žúľŽ°ú
  Žā®Í≤®ŽĎźžóąžĚĆ. Í≤įÍ≥ľž†ĀžúľŽ°ú žā¨žö©žěźŽäĒ LLM žĚĎŽčĶžóźžĄú `\[ \frac{1}{m} \sum_{i=1}
  ^{m} \ell(\alpha_i) \]` ÍįôžĚÄ raw LaTeX Ž•ľ Í∑łŽĆÄŽ°ú Ž≥īÍ≥† žěąžóąŽč§. Ž≥ł PR žĚī
  ŽĎź Íį≠žĚĄ ŽčęžĚĆ:
  - `core/cli/interactive_loop.py` žĚė `_render_ipc_response` ÍįÄ LLM final
    text Ž•ľ `rich.markdown.Markdown` žúľŽ°ú žßĀž†Ď ŪĚėŽ¶¨Žćė Ž∂ÄŽ∂ĄžĚĄ žč†Í∑ú
    `_render_text_with_latex` Ūó¨ŪćľŽ°ú ÍĶźž≤ī. Ūó¨ŪćľŽäĒ
    `extract_and_render_inline(text)` Ž°ú segment Ž∂ĄŪē† ŪõĄ inline math ŽäĒ
    rendered Unicode Ž°ú ž£ľŽ≥Ä Markdown paragraph žóź Žč§žčú Ūē©žĻėÍ≥†,
    `block_math` ŽäĒ multi-line block žúľŽ°ú render. math ÍįÄ ž†ĄŪėÄ žóÜžúľŽ©ī
    Žč®žĚľ Markdown Ūėłž∂úŽ°ú fallback (ŪöĆÍ∑Ä žúĄŪóė 0).
  - `core/ui/latex.py` žĚė delimiter ÍįÄ `$...$` / `$$...$$` ŽĎź ÍįÄžßÄ ŽŅźžĚīŽĚľ
    LLM žĚī žěźž£ľ ž∂úŽ†•ŪēėŽäĒ `\[...\]` (display) / `\(...\)` (inline) /
    `\begin{equation|align|gather|multline|displaymath}‚Ä¶\end{...}` ÍįÄ
    Ž™®ŽĎź ŽąĄŽĚĹ. Ž≥ł PR žĚī žĄł ŪĆ®ŪĄī Ž™®ŽĎź žßÄžõźŪēėŽŹĄŽ°Ě regex Ūôēžě• + overlap-
    aware žöįžĄ†žąúžúĄ resolution (block > inline) ž∂ĒÍįÄ.
  - žč†Í∑ú test 13 (`tests/test_ui_latex.py::TestDelimiterExpansion` 7 +
    `tests/test_interactive_loop_latex.py` 6) ‚ÄĒ Ž™®Žď† delimiter form,
    mixed segments, overlap ŪöĆŪĒľ, raw ŽįĪžä¨Žěėžčú leak ŪöĆÍ∑Ä, žā¨žö©žěźÍįÄ Ž≥īÍ≥†Ūēú
    `\[ \frac{1}{m} \sum_{i=1}^{m} \ell(\alpha_i) \]` žľÄžĚīžä§ žßĀž†Ď Í≤Äž¶Ě.
  - žĚėŽŹĄŽźú ŽĻĄžßÄžõź: backslash žóÜŽäĒ `[...]` / `(...)` ‚ÄĒ markdown link
    Ž¨łŽ≤ēÍ≥ľ ž∂©ŽŹĆ + žĚľŽįė bracket žĖīŪúė noise. žā¨žö©žěźŽäĒ `\[...\]` ŪėēžčĚžĚĄ žć®žēľ
    Ūē®.
- **CLI LaTeX rendering ‚ÄĒ `interactive_loop` wiring + `\[...\]`/`\(...\)`/
  `\begin{env}‚Ä¶\end{env}` delimiter support.** PR #1141 introduced
  `core/ui/latex.py` with the Tier 1 (pylatexenc) and Tier 2
  (latex2sympy2 + sympy.pretty) renderers plus 19 tests, but the
  CHANGELOG flagged the actual wiring as a follow-up ‚ÄĒ the response
  print path stayed on `rich.markdown.Markdown(text)`. Users therefore
  saw the raw backslash form (e.g. `\[ \frac{1}{m} \sum_{i=1}^{m}
  \ell(\alpha_i) \]`) in their terminals. This PR closes both gaps:
  - The LLM final-text branch of
    `core/cli/interactive_loop._render_ipc_response` now calls a new
    `_render_text_with_latex` helper. The helper splits the body via
    `extract_and_render_inline(text)`, folds inline math back into the
    surrounding Markdown paragraph as rendered Unicode, and renders
    `block_math` as a multi-line block. When the body has no math at
    all, it falls back to the single Markdown call (zero regression
    risk).
  - `core/ui/latex.py` only knew `$...$` and `$$...$$`. The new regex
    set adds the three forms LLMs actually emit ‚ÄĒ `\[‚Ä¶\]` for
    display, `\(‚Ä¶\)` for inline, and `\begin{equation|align|gather|
    multline|displaymath}‚Ä¶\end{...}` ‚ÄĒ with overlap-aware priority
    resolution (block > inline) so an inline match inside a
    multi-line bracket block is not double-extracted.
  - 13 new tests
    (`tests/test_ui_latex.py::TestDelimiterExpansion` plus
    `tests/test_interactive_loop_latex.py`) pin every delimiter form,
    mixed segments, the overlap rule, the raw-backslash leak
    regression, and the user-reported case verbatim.
  - Deliberately not supported: bracket forms without backslashes
    (`[...]` / `(...)`) ‚ÄĒ those collide with Markdown link syntax and
    ordinary parenthetical prose. Users must write `\[‚Ä¶\]`.

- **`docs-link-audit` skill registered.** Added

  - **anchor #section** ‚ÄĒ ÍįôžĚÄ page.tsx žĚė `id="..."` žôÄ ŽĆÄž°į
  - **external http(s)://** ‚ÄĒ `--http` žėĶŪäłžĚł žčú HEAD/GET žúľŽ°ú reachability
    Í≤Äžā¨ (concurrent 8, 8s timeout, 200/3xx OK)
  CI ŪÜĶŪē© žėĶžÖė: `python3 scripts/check_docs_links.py` ŽßĆžúľŽ°ú ž†ēž†Ā Í≤Äžā¨
  ŪÜĶÍ≥ľ žčú exit 0, broken ŽįúÍ≤¨ žčú exit 1. ŪĖ•ŪõĄ pages.yml build job žĚė
  pre-build step ŽėźŽäĒ Ž≥Ą ci.yml lint žúľŽ°ú wiring ÍįÄŽä•.

  ŪėĄžě¨ žł°ž†ē (žĚī PR ž†Āžö© ŪõĄ): 193 link žä§žļĒ, 0 broken, 17 external Ž™®ŽĎź
  reachable, 2 unresolved (markdown-lite.tsx žĚė regex ŪĆ®ŪĄī Ž¨łžěźžóī, false
  positive Ž¨īžčú).
- **`scripts/check_docs_links.py` ‚ÄĒ static + HTTP audit script for docs
  site links.** Extracts every link-shaped string from `site/src/**.tsx`
  / `.ts` (JSX `href="..."`, ``href={`...`}``, `src="..."`, `to="..."`,
  and markdown-style link syntax inside string literals). Classifies
  into four buckets:
  - **internal /docs/...** ‚ÄĒ diffed against the `page.tsx` slug set
    under `site/src/app/docs/`; misses flagged.
  - **internal /\<other\>...** ‚ÄĒ `/portfolio`, `/works`,
    `/petri-bundle/` etc., diffed against app routes + public assets +
    build-time copies (the script recognises the
    `docs/petri-bundle/` ‚Üí `site/out/petri-bundle/` copy step in
    `.github/workflows/pages.yml`).
  - **anchor #section** ‚ÄĒ checked against `id="..."` occurrences in
    the emitting `page.tsx`.
  - **external http(s)://** ‚ÄĒ opt-in `--http` HEAD/GET probe
    (concurrency 8, 8 s timeout, 200/3xx considered OK).
  Returns exit 0 on a clean run and exit 1 if anything is broken, so
  the script can later be wired into the Pages build (pre-build step)
  or into the `ci.yml` lint job. Current measurement after this PR:
  193 link occurrences scanned, 0 broken, all 17 external URLs
  reachable, 2 false-positive "unresolved" entries from regex pattern
  strings in `markdown-lite.tsx` (informational, not failure).

  `find site/src/app/docs -name "page.tsx"` žĚė 50 Íįú žč§žě¨ ŪéėžĚīžßÄ žä¨Žü¨Í∑łžôÄ
  `comm -23` žúľŽ°ú žį®žßĎŪē© ‚Üí 3 broken ŽįúÍ≤¨. `npm run build` žĄĪÍ≥Ķ ŪõĄ 6 žā¨žĚīŪäł

- **Docs site broken-link fix ‚ÄĒ 3 paths (6 sites).** A static scan of
  internal links in `site/src/` found three routes that returned 404
  because the target slug either no longer exists (an earlier `build/`
  chapter was retired) or was renamed to another section:
  - `/docs/build/add-domain` ‚Üí `/docs/runtime/domains`
    (`run/analyze/page.tsx` L38, L65).
  - `/docs/build/add-tool` ‚Üí `/docs/runtime/tools/protocol`
    (`run/messaging/page.tsx` L35, L60).
  - `/docs/ops/observability` ‚Üí `/docs/verification/observability`
    (`petri/run/page.tsx` L77, L146).

  Detection used `grep` against `site/src/` for all `href="(/docs/...)"`,
  ``href={`/docs/...`}``, and markdown-style `](/docs/...)` patterns
  (23 unique link targets) cross-referenced via `comm -23` against the
  50 page-slug paths under `site/src/app/docs/**/page.tsx`; the
  difference produced exactly 3 broken targets. `npm run build` passes
  after the 6 edits. Documentation only ‚ÄĒ no behavioural impact.

### Added

- **Autoresearch real-mode runtime hook (`GEODE_WRAPPER_OVERRIDE`).**
  `core/llm/prompt_assembler.py` žĚė `assemble()` žóź Phase 0 (Wrapper
  Override) ž∂ĒÍįÄ. env var `GEODE_WRAPPER_OVERRIDE=<json-path>` ÍįÄ set
  ŽźėŽ©ī JSON žĚĄ `dict[str, str]` Ž°ú Ž°úŽďúŪēī Í∑ł value Žď§žĚĄ concat Ūēú Í≤įÍ≥ľŽ°ú
  `base_system` žĚĄ ŽĆÄž≤ī. ŪõĄžÜć Phase (skill / memory / extra) ŽäĒ Í∑łŽĆÄŽ°ú
  ž†Āžö©. env unset žĚÄ baseline žĚĄ žú†žßÄŪēėžßÄŽßĆ, env ÍįÄ set Žźú Ží§ ŪĆĆžĚľ ŽąĄŽĚĹ /
  malformed JSON / dict žēĄŽčė / empty dict / non-string entry ÍįÄ Žāėžė§Ž©ī
  fail-closed `RuntimeError` Ž°ú real audit quota Ž•ľ baseline prompt žóź
  žďįžßÄ žēäÍ≤Ć Ūē®. `autoresearch/train.py` žĚė
  `WRAPPER_OVERRIDE_HOOK_READY` Ž•ľ `True` Ž°ú flip Ūēī real-mode ŪôúžĄĪŪôĒ ‚ÄĒ
  outer-loop agent ÍįÄ `WRAPPER_PROMPT_SECTIONS` Ž•ľ žąėž†ēŪēėŽ©ī `geode audit`
  žĚė system prompt ÍįÄ žč§ž†úŽ°ú Í∑ł dict žĚė Žāīžö©žúľŽ°ú ŽŹôžěĎ. `.env.example` žóź
  `# GEODE_WRAPPER_OVERRIDE=` Ūē≠Ž™© + žā¨žö© žĄ§Ž™Ö ž∂ĒÍįÄ. žč†Í∑ú 9 pytest
  (`tests/test_prompt_assembler.py` žĚė `TestWrapperOverrideHook` ‚ÄĒ
  env-unset baseline / ž†ēžÉĀ override / ŪĆĆžĚľ ŽąĄŽĚĹ raise / malformed
  JSON raise / ŽĻĄ-dict raise / empty dict raise / non-string entry raise /
  hash ÍīÄžł°žĄĪ / extra Ūē©žĄĪ)
  + train.py žĚė fail-fast test Ž•ľ real-mode subprocess argv/env Í≤Äž¶Ě
  žúľŽ°ú ÍĶźž≤ī (mock subprocess, quota žā¨žö© žóÜžĚĆ).
- **Autoresearch real-mode runtime hook (`GEODE_WRAPPER_OVERRIDE`).**
  Adds Phase 0 (Wrapper Override) to `core/llm/prompt_assembler.py`'s
  `assemble()`. When `GEODE_WRAPPER_OVERRIDE=<json-path>` is set, the
  JSON is loaded as `dict[str, str]` and its values are concatenated to
  replace `base_system`; the remaining phases (skill / memory / extra)
  still apply on top. When the env is unset, baseline behavior is
  unchanged; once the env is set, missing files, malformed JSON,
  non-dict payloads, empty dicts, or non-string entries fail closed with
  `RuntimeError` so real audit quota is not spent on the baseline prompt.
  `autoresearch/train.py` flips
  `WRAPPER_OVERRIDE_HOOK_READY = True`, enabling real-mode runs ‚ÄĒ the
  outer-loop agent's edits to `WRAPPER_PROMPT_SECTIONS` now actually
  reach the `geode audit` system prompt. `.env.example` documents the
  new optional variable. Nine new pytest cases in
  `tests/test_prompt_assembler.py::TestWrapperOverrideHook` (baseline /
  override / missing file raises / malformed JSON raises / non-dict raises /
  empty dict raises / non-string entries raise / hash observability /
  composition with bootstrap extras) plus
  the existing `tests/test_autoresearch_train.py` fail-fast test
  replaced by a real-mode subprocess argv/env assertion (subprocess
  mocked ‚ÄĒ no LLM quota consumed).

- **Phase 1a ‚ÄĒ Long-term Recall: messages table + dual-write.** Hermes
  ŪĚ°žąė plan(`docs/plans/2026-05-14-hermes-strengths-absorption.md`) žĚė ž≤ę
  PR. `sessions.db` žóź `messages` ŪÖĆžĚīŽłĒ (id / session_id / seq / role /
  content / tool_call_id / tool_calls / tool_name / timestamp /
  token_count / finish_reason / reasoning / metadata + `UNIQUE(session_id,
  seq)`) + `idx_messages_session` + `idx_messages_tool_name` žč†žĄ§.
  `SessionCheckpoint.save()` ÍįÄ JSON Ž≥łŽ¨ł ž†Äžě• žßĀŪõĄ
  `SessionManager.upsert_messages()` Ž°ú Ž≥łŽ¨łžĚĄ mirror ‚ÄĒ JSON žĚÄ Phase 1b
  žĚė SoT ž†ĄŪôėÍĻĆžßÄ authoritative. DB žč§ŪĆ® žčú WARN Ž°úÍĻÖ + `exc_info=True`,
  JSON Ž≥łŽ¨łžĚÄ Í∑łŽĆÄŽ°ú Ž≥īž°ī (graceful degradation). ŽŹôžĚľ/ž∂ēžÜĆ/ŽĻą message
  list žĚė žě¨ž†Äžě• Ž™®ŽĎź idempotent ‚ÄĒ ž§ĄžĖīŽď† seq žĚė stale row žôÄ ŽĻą ž†Äžě•žĚė
  žěĒžó¨ row ÍĻĆžßÄ ž†úÍĪįŪēī JSON ‚ÜĒ DB ÍįÄ Ūē≠žÉĀ ž†ēŽ†¨. Anthropic content blocks
  (`tool_use` / `tool_result` / `thinking`) žôÄ OpenAI ŪėēžčĚ (`tool_calls`
  / `tool_call_id` / `name`) žĖĎž™Ĺ ž∂Ēž∂ú + 18 žč†Í∑ú ŪÖĆžä§Ūäł (dual-write
  parity / sqlite žč§ŪĆ® graceful / openai+anthropic ž∂Ēž∂ú / stale row
  ž†úÍĪį / ŽĻą ž†Äžě• ž†ēŪē©). Codex MCP cross-LLM verifier ÍįÄ CRITICAL 2 ÍĪī
  (stale row + ŽĻą-save žěĒžě¨) žĚĄ ŽįúÍ≤¨¬∑ŽįėžėĀ.
- **Phase 1a ‚ÄĒ Long-term Recall: messages table + dual-write.** First PR
  of the Hermes-absorption plan (`docs/plans/2026-05-14-hermes-strengths-
  absorption.md`). Adds a `messages` table to `sessions.db` (columns: id
  / session_id / seq / role / content / tool_call_id / tool_calls /
  tool_name / timestamp / token_count / finish_reason / reasoning /
  metadata + `UNIQUE(session_id, seq)`) plus `idx_messages_session` and
  `idx_messages_tool_name`. `SessionCheckpoint.save()` mirrors the full
  message list into the table right after the JSON write, via
  `SessionManager.upsert_messages()`; JSON remains SoT until Phase 1b
  flips the source. DB failures emit a WARNING with `exc_info=True` and
  leave the JSON checkpoint intact (graceful degradation). Re-saving the
  same, shorter, or empty message list is idempotent ‚ÄĒ stale rows from a
  shrunk transcript and leftovers from an empty save are removed so JSON
  and the mirror stay aligned. The extractor reads both Anthropic content
  blocks (`tool_use` / `tool_result` / `thinking`) and OpenAI-style
  fields (`tool_calls` / `tool_call_id` / `name`). 18 new tests cover
  dual-write parity, a real `sqlite3.OperationalError` graceful path,
  OpenAI/Anthropic extraction, stale-row removal, and empty-save
  alignment. A Codex MCP cross-LLM verifier round caught two CRITICAL
  gaps (stale rows on shrink, leftovers on empty save), both fixed.

### Changed

  ŽĆÄÍłį) žĚĄ ŪŹźÍłįŪēėÍ≥† Karpathy
  [autoresearch](https://github.com/karpathy/autoresearch) (228791f, MIT,
  26K+ stars) žĚė 3-file ŪĆ®ŪĄī (`prepare.py` + `train.py` + `program.md`)
  žúľŽ°ú žě¨ÍĶ¨žĄĪ. ML ŽŹĄŽ©ĒžĚł (GPT pre-train + `val_bpb`) žĚĄ GEODE alignment-
  audit ŽŹĄŽ©ĒžĚł (Petri seed pool + AlphaEval 5-axis fitness) žúľŽ°ú žĶúžÜĆ
  ÍĶźž≤ī. Karpathy žõźŽ≥łžĚė single-mutation-file + fixed-budget + grep-friendly
  stdout + `results.tsv` 5-col + git-as-optimizer ž†ēžč† žú†žßÄ.
  - `autoresearch/prepare.py` ‚ÄĒ `~/.cache/autoresearch/` žĚė fineweb Žč§žöī
    Ž°úŽďú + tokenizer ŪēôžäĶ žěźŽ¶¨žóź **fixed audit harness sanity check**
    (10 safe seed file count, 19-dim YAML rubric parse, `geode audit

    fallback.
  - `autoresearch/train.py` ‚ÄĒ GPT ŪēôžäĶ žěźŽ¶¨žóź **wrapper system-prompt
    section dict** (mutation target) + `geode audit` subprocess invocation
    (staged: `--seed-select` / `--dim-set` / `--live` / `--yes`) + 5-axis
    fitness extraction. ŪėĄžě¨ GEODE core ÍįÄ `GEODE_WRAPPER_OVERRIDE` Ž•ľ
    žēĄžßĀ consume ŪēėžßÄ žēäžúľŽĮÄŽ°ú real mode ŽäĒ fail-fast (`RuntimeError`),
    `--dry-run` ŽßĆ working mode ‚ÄĒ runtime hook žĚÄ follow-up PR.
  - `autoresearch/program.md` ‚ÄĒ ML research direction žěźŽ¶¨žóź Petri
    direction (gen 0 hypothesis space, `input_hallucination` őĒ +1.13 /
    `overrefusal` +0.31 / `broken_tool_use` ‚ąí1.28 driver seed žĚė prior,
    9 hypothesis = wrapper section ablation).
  - `autoresearch/README.md` ‚ÄĒ Petri-signal fork žĚė quick start.
  - `pyproject.toml` žĚė `geode-research = "autoresearch.loop:cli"`
    entry-point ž†úÍĪį (Karpathy žõźŽ≥ł ž†ēžč†: single-script `uv run python
    autoresearch/train.py`, CLI wrapping žóÜžĚĆ).
  - 3 žč†Í∑ú pytest (`tests/test_autoresearch_train.py`) ‚ÄĒ argv ÍįÄ ŪėĄžě¨
    `geode audit` flag ŽßĆ žā¨žö© + žā¨ŽĚľžßĄ flag žĚė retro ŪöĆŪĒľ + real-mode
    fail-fast + dry-run baseline žĚė fitness range Í≤Äž¶Ě.
  - Í∑łŽĚľžöīŽĒ© reference clone: `~/workspace/autoresearch` (Žč®žąú clone,
    GEODE repo žôÄ git žóįŽŹô žóÜžĚĆ).
  - Codex MCP cross-LLM verifier ÍįÄ CRITICAL 1 + HIGH 4 žěźŽŹô fix ‚ÄĒ
    real-mode deception, obsolete CLI flag, rubric grep žĚė stale ŪėēžčĚ,
    `~/.cache` Í∂ĆŪēú fallback.
- **Autoresearch outer-loop: Karpathy 3-file fork (Petri-signal domain).**
  Retires the 6-module Python stub from PR #1145 (`loop.py` /
  `hypothesis.py` / `fitness.py` / `ratchet.py` / `rationale_extractor.py`
  / `baseline_marker.py`, 480 LOC, "follow-up PR1" placeholder) and
  rebases the outer loop on the Karpathy
  [autoresearch](https://github.com/karpathy/autoresearch) 3-file pattern
  (228791f, MIT, 26K+ stars). Domain swapped from ML pre-training
  (`val_bpb`) to GEODE alignment-audit (Petri seed pool + AlphaEval
  5-axis fitness), preserving Karpathy's single-mutation-file +
  fixed-budget + grep-friendly stdout + `results.tsv` 5-col +
  git-as-optimizer spirit.
  - `autoresearch/prepare.py` replaces fineweb download + tokenizer
    training with a **fixed audit harness sanity check** (seed file
    count, 19-dim YAML rubric parse, `geode audit --help` reachability),
    and falls back to a worktree-local report if `~/.cache` is read-only.
  - `autoresearch/train.py` replaces the GPT training loop with a
    **wrapper system-prompt section dict** (the agent's mutation surface)
    plus a `geode audit` subprocess invocation (staged: `--seed-select` /
    `--dim-set` / `--live` / `--yes`) and 5-axis fitness extraction.
    Until GEODE core consumes `GEODE_WRAPPER_OVERRIDE`, real mode
    fail-fasts and only `--dry-run` is non-deceptive; the runtime hook
    lands in a follow-up PR.
  - `autoresearch/program.md` replaces the ML research direction with a
    Petri direction (gen-0 hypothesis space drawn from yesterday's
    driver seeds: `input_hallucination` őĒ +1.13 / `overrefusal` +0.31 /
    `broken_tool_use` ‚ąí1.28 across three model families, pruning the
    space to nine wrapper-section ablations).
  - `autoresearch/README.md` ships a Petri-signal fork quick-start.
  - `pyproject.toml` drops the `geode-research = "autoresearch.loop:cli"`
    entry-point ‚ÄĒ Karpathy keeps the runner as a plain `python train.py`,
    so the fork does too.
  - Three new pytest tests (`tests/test_autoresearch_train.py`) pin the
    current `geode audit` flag set, block obsolete flags from creeping
    back in, assert the real-mode fail-fast, and check the dry-run
    fitness range.
  - Grounding reference clone: `~/workspace/autoresearch` (a plain
    clone, not wired into the GEODE repo).
  - Codex MCP acted as a cross-LLM verifier and applied one CRITICAL fix
    plus four HIGH fixes ‚ÄĒ real-mode deception, obsolete CLI flags,
    stale rubric grep, and the `~/.cache` permission fallback.

### Fixed

- **Autoresearch Petri scaffold verifier fixes.** `prepare.py` now parses the
  19-dimension YAML rubric instead of grepping for a stale `- name:` shape,
  falls back to a workspace-local prepare report when `~/.cache` is not
  writable, and `train.py` fail-fast blocks real audit mode until GEODE core
  actually consumes `GEODE_WRAPPER_OVERRIDE`. The staged live argv now matches
  the current `geode audit` CLI (`--seed-select`, `--dim-set`, `--live`,
  `--yes`) instead of obsolete `--rubric` / `--budget-minutes` flags.
- **Autoresearch Petri scaffold Í≤Äž¶Ě žąėž†ē.** `prepare.py` ÍįÄ žė§ŽěėŽźú

  `~/.cache` žóź žďł žąė žóÜžĚĄ ŽēĆ worktree-local prepare report Ž°ú fallback
  Ūē©ŽčąŽč§. `train.py` ŽäĒ GEODE core ÍįÄ `GEODE_WRAPPER_OVERRIDE` Ž•ľ žč§ž†úŽ°ú
  consume ŪēėÍłį ž†ĄÍĻĆžßÄ real audit mode Ž•ľ fail-fast Ž°ú ŽßČžēĄ, wrapper mutation
  žĚī ž†Āžö©ŽźėŽäĒ Í≤Éž≤ėŽüľ Ž≥īžĚīŽäĒ žį©žčúŽ•ľ ž†úÍĪįŪĖąžäĶŽčąŽč§. staged live argv ŽŹĄ ŪėĄžě¨
  `geode audit` CLI žĚė `--seed-select`, `--dim-set`, `--live`, `--yes` žóź
  Žßěž∑ĄžäĶŽčąŽč§.
